---
title: "Mormyrid Gut Content Analysis: Dietary Niche Partitioning and EOD Waveform Polymorphism"
author: "Gorsuch, Picq, Gallant"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
    keep_tex: false
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    code_folding: hide
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
header-includes:
- \usepackage{longtable}
- \usepackage{booktabs}
- \usepackage{float}
- \usepackage{array}
- \usepackage{pdflscape}
---

# Overview

This analysis examines dietary niche partitioning in mormyrid electric fishes across four hierarchical biological levels, with a focus on the relationship between EOD waveform polymorphism and diet in *Paramormyrops kingsleyae*.

**Analytical Framework:**

1. **LEVEL 1:** Mormyrid Community at Balé Creek (9 species, 35 individuals, single site)
2. **LEVEL 2:** BP vs. TP Mormyrids Globally (~97-99 individuals across all sites)
3. **LEVEL 3:** *P. kingsleyae* General Population Factors (~47 individuals from 4 rivers)
4. **LEVEL 4:** BP vs. TP Within *P. kingsleyae* (The Core Question)
5. **LEVEL 5:** Cross-Validation Across Primer Sets

**Primer Sets:**

| Set | Forward | Reverse | Name |
|-----|---------|---------|------|
| PS1 | mlCOIintF | BR2 | Primerset 1 |
| PS2 | BF2 | BR1 | Primerset 2 |

All analyses are conducted on both primer sets in parallel to ensure biological robustness.

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Configure knitr
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 7,   # or 8                                                                                                                                                   
  fig.height = 4.5 ,# or 5   
  dpi = 300
)

# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)      # Data manipulation and visualization
library(qiime2R)        # QIIME2 data import
library(phyloseq)       # Microbiome analysis framework
library(microbiome)     # Microbiome utilities
library(vegan)          # Ecological statistics
library(pairwiseAdonis) # Pairwise PERMANOVA tests
library(spaa)           # Diet overlap indices
library(patchwork)      # Multi-panel figures
library(knitr)          # Table formatting
library(kableExtra)     # Enhanced tables
library(ape)            # Phylogenetic analysis
library(effsize)        # Effect size calculations
library(stringdist)     # String distance calculations for fuzzy matching

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Set root path
root <- rprojroot::find_root(".git/index")

# Source helper functions
source(file.path(root, "code/07_Analysis/gut_analysis_2026_helpers.R"))

# Color palettes (colorblind-friendly)
# Waveform colors
bp_color <- "#0072B2"      # Blue for BP
tp_color <- "#D55E00"      # Orange for TP

# Region colors
north_color <- "#009E73"   # Green for North
south_color <- "#CC79A7"   # Purple for South

# Primer set colors
ps1_color <- "#E69F00"     # Orange
ps2_color <- "#56B4E9"     # Sky blue

# Selectivity colors
preference_color <- "#D55E00"   # Orange
neutral_color <- "#F0E442"      # Yellow
avoidance_color <- "#0072B2"    # Blue

# Set global ggplot2 theme with sans-serif font and publication-quality sizes
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "sans"))
theme_update(
  axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
  strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
  plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
)
```

---

# Data Loading and Preparation

Load phyloseq objects for both primer sets, filter host sequences, and rarefy to common depth.

```{r load-data}
cat("=== LOADING PRIMER SET DATA ===\n\n")

# Load both primer sets with host filtering
ps1 <- load_primerset_data(1, use_no_host = TRUE)
ps2 <- load_primerset_data(2, use_no_host = TRUE)

cat("\n=== INITIAL DATASET SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Primer Set 1:", nsamples(ps1), "samples,", ntaxa(ps1), "ASVs\n")
cat("Primer Set 2:", nsamples(ps2), "samples,", ntaxa(ps2), "ASVs\n")

# Check rarefaction depths
cat("\nRarefaction depths:\n")
cat("  PS1:", unique(sample_sums(ps1)), "\n")
cat("  PS2:", unique(sample_sums(ps2)), "\n")

# Initialize global color palette for consistent family colors across all plots
cat("\n=== CREATING GLOBAL COLOR PALETTE ===\n")
GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS <- create_global_family_palette(ps1, ps2, min_families = 50)
cat("Created color palette for", length(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS) - 1, "families + Other\n")
```

## Sample Matching and Metadata Preparation

```{r sample-matching}
# Get sample names for both primer sets
ps1_samples <- sample_data(ps1)$Sample_Name
ps2_samples <- sample_data(ps2)$Sample_Name

# Find shared samples
shared_samples <- intersect(ps1_samples, ps2_samples)

cat("=== SAMPLE OVERLAP ===\n")
cat("PS1 total samples:", length(ps1_samples), "\n")
cat("PS2 total samples:", length(ps2_samples), "\n")
cat("Shared samples:", length(shared_samples), "\n")
cat("Overlap proportion:",
    round(length(shared_samples) / length(union(ps1_samples, ps2_samples)), 3), "\n")

# Extract metadata (will be same for both after matching)
meta_all <- data.frame(sample_data(ps1), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Create species-level scientific names
meta_all$Species <- clean_species_name(meta_all$Species)

# Display sample sizes by species and river
sample_summary <- meta_all %>%
  group_by(Species, Locality_Name) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Locality_Name, values_from = n, values_fill = list(n = 0)) %>%
  mutate(Total = rowSums(select(., -Species))) %>%
  bind_rows(
    summarise(., across(where(is.numeric), sum), Species = "Total")
  )

kable(sample_summary,
      caption = "Sample sizes by species and river",
      align = c("l", rep("r", ncol(sample_summary) - 1)),
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(nrow(sample_summary), bold = TRUE)
```

## Create Subsets for Different Analytical Levels

```{r create-subsets}
cat("\n=== CREATING ANALYTICAL SUBSETS ===\n\n")

# LEVEL 1: Balé Creek only (9 species)
ps1_bale <- subset_samples(ps1, Locality_Name == "Bale Creek")
ps2_bale <- subset_samples(ps2, Locality_Name == "Bale Creek")

# Remove rare taxa (present in <2 samples or <10 total reads)
ps1_bale <- filter_taxa(ps1_bale, function(x) sum(x > 0) >= 2 & sum(x) >= 10, TRUE)
ps2_bale <- filter_taxa(ps2_bale, function(x) sum(x > 0) >= 2 & sum(x) >= 10, TRUE)

cat("LEVEL 1 - Balé Creek:\n")
cat("  PS1:", nsamples(ps1_bale), "samples,", ntaxa(ps1_bale), "ASVs\n")
cat("  PS2:", nsamples(ps2_bale), "samples,", ntaxa(ps2_bale), "ASVs\n")

# LEVEL 2: All mormyrids (BP vs TP globally)
ps1_all_mormyrids <- ps1
ps2_all_mormyrids <- ps2

# Add waveform type metadata (BP or TP)
# Note: This assumes Waveform_Type column exists in metadata
cat("\nLEVEL 2 - All Mormyrids (BP vs TP):\n")
cat("  PS1:", nsamples(ps1_all_mormyrids), "samples\n")
cat("  PS2:", nsamples(ps2_all_mormyrids), "samples\n")

waveform_counts <- table(sample_data(ps1)$Waveform_Type)
cat("  BP samples:", waveform_counts["BP"], "\n")
cat("  TP samples:", waveform_counts["TP"], "\n")

# LEVEL 3 & 4: P. kingsleyae only
ps1_pk <- subset_samples(ps1, Species == "Paramormyrops kingsleyae")
ps2_pk <- subset_samples(ps2, Species == "Paramormyrops kingsleyae")

# Remove rare taxa
ps1_pk <- filter_taxa(ps1_pk, function(x) sum(x > 0) >= 2 & sum(x) >= 10, TRUE)
ps2_pk <- filter_taxa(ps2_pk, function(x) sum(x > 0) >= 2 & sum(x) >= 10, TRUE)

cat("\nLEVEL 3/4 - P. kingsleyae:\n")
cat("  PS1:", nsamples(ps1_pk), "samples,", ntaxa(ps1_pk), "ASVs\n")
cat("  PS2:", nsamples(ps2_pk), "samples,", ntaxa(ps2_pk), "ASVs\n")

# Check P. kingsleyae sampling design
pk_meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps1_pk))
pk_design <- pk_meta %>%
  group_by(Locality_Name, Waveform_Type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Waveform_Type, values_from = n, values_fill = list(n = 0))

cat("\nP. kingsleyae sampling design:\n")
print(pk_design)
```

## Load Phylogenetic Data

```{r load-phylogeny}
cat("\n=== LOADING PHYLOGENETIC DATA ===\n\n")

# Load phylogenetic tree
tree_file <- file.path(root, "input_data/ssAll_iqtree_timtree.tree")

if (file.exists(tree_file)) {
  # Read tree - NEXUS format may create multiple trees, extract the real one
  phylo_tree_raw <- read.tree(tree_file)

  # If multiPhylo object, extract the second tree (the actual phylogeny)
  if (inherits(phylo_tree_raw, "multiPhylo")) {
    phylo_tree <- phylo_tree_raw[[2]]
    cat("Note: NEXUS file contained", length(phylo_tree_raw),
        "trees, extracted tree 2\n\n")
  } else {
    phylo_tree <- phylo_tree_raw
  }

  cat("Phylogenetic tree loaded:\n")
  cat("  Tips:", length(phylo_tree$tip.label), "\n")
  cat("  Tree type:", ifelse(is.ultrametric(phylo_tree), "Ultrametric",
                              "Non-ultrametric"), "\n")

  # Create cophenetic distance matrix
  phylo_dist <- cophenetic(phylo_tree)

  cat("\nPhylogenetic distances computed\n")
  cat("  Dimensions:", dim(phylo_dist)[1], "x", dim(phylo_dist)[2], "\n")

  # Show relevant tree tips for debugging
  cat("\nRelevant tree tips by genus:\n")
  param_tips <- grep("Paramormyrops", phylo_tree$tip.label, value = TRUE)
  morm_tips <- grep("^Mormyrops", phylo_tree$tip.label, value = TRUE)
  marc_tips <- grep("Marcusenius", phylo_tree$tip.label, value = TRUE)
  petro_tips <- grep("Petrocephalus", phylo_tree$tip.label, value = TRUE)

  cat("\nParamormyrops (", length(param_tips), " tips):\n", sep = "")
  cat(paste(param_tips, collapse = "\n"), "\n")

  cat("\nMormyrops (", length(morm_tips), " tips):\n", sep = "")
  cat(paste(morm_tips, collapse = "\n"), "\n")

  cat("\nMarcusenius (", length(marc_tips), " tips):\n", sep = "")
  cat(paste(marc_tips, collapse = "\n"), "\n")

  cat("\nPetrocephalus (", length(petro_tips), " tips, showing first 5):\n", sep = "")
  cat(paste(head(petro_tips, 5), collapse = "\n"), "\n")

  # Fuzzy match Balé species to phylogeny
  match_results <- match_bale_species_to_phylogeny(ps1_bale, phylo_tree)

  cat("\nBalé Creek species matching:\n")
  cat("  Successfully matched:", match_results$n_matched, "/", match_results$n_species, "\n\n")

  # Print detailed results
  print(match_results$match_table)

  # Extract matched tips for downstream use
  matched_tips <- match_results$matched_species
  if (length(matched_tips) > 0) {
    phylo_dist_bale <- phylo_dist[matched_tips, matched_tips]
  }

} else {
  cat("WARNING: Phylogenetic tree file not found at:\n")
  cat("  ", tree_file, "\n")
  phylo_tree <- NULL
  phylo_dist <- NULL
}
```

## Helper Functions

Additional helper functions specific to this analysis.

```{r additional-helpers}
#' Aggregate phyloseq object to family level
#'
#' @param ps phyloseq object
#' @return phyloseq object aggregated to family level
aggregate_to_family <- function(ps) {
  ps_family <- tax_glom(ps, taxrank = "Family", NArm = FALSE)
  return(ps_family)
}


#' Create stacked bar chart of prey composition at family level
#'
#' @param ps phyloseq object (should be aggregated to family level)
#' @param group_var Character, grouping variable for x-axis
#' @param top_n Integer, number of top families to show (rest as "Other")
#' @param title Character, plot title
#' @return ggplot object
plot_composition_bars <- function(ps, group_var, top_n = 10, title = "") {

  # Melt to long format (before normalization)
  ps_melt <- psmelt(ps)

  # Clean family names
  ps_melt$Family_clean <- sapply(ps_melt$Family, clean_family_name)

  # Aggregate by group_var and Family, summing counts
  ps_agg <- ps_melt %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]], Family_clean) %>%
    summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

  # Normalize within each group to get relative abundance
  ps_agg <- ps_agg %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]]) %>%
    mutate(RelAbund = Abundance / sum(Abundance)) %>%
    ungroup()

  # Identify top N families by mean relative abundance across groups
  top_families <- ps_agg %>%
    group_by(Family_clean) %>%
    summarise(mean_abund = mean(RelAbund), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_abund)) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n) %>%
    pull(Family_clean)

  # Categorize families and aggregate "Other"
  ps_final <- ps_agg %>%
    mutate(Family_display = ifelse(Family_clean %in% top_families,
                                   Family_clean, "Other")) %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]], Family_display) %>%
    summarise(RelAbund = sum(RelAbund), .groups = "drop")

  # Reorder for plotting (top families first, then Other)
  ps_final$Family_display <- factor(ps_final$Family_display,
                                     levels = c(top_families, "Other"))

  # Create plot with consistent color scale
  p <- ggplot(ps_final, aes(x = .data[[group_var]], y = RelAbund,
                            fill = Family_display)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS,
                      name = "Prey Family",
                      na.value = "#808080",
                      drop = FALSE) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.key.size = unit(0.35, "cm"),
          legend.box = "vertical") +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 4, title.position = "top",
                               byrow = TRUE, nrow = 3)) +
    labs(title = title, x = group_var, y = "Relative Abundance") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)

  return(p)
}


#' Variance partitioning for PERMANOVA
#'
#' @param beta_result Beta diversity result from run_beta_diversity_analysis()
#' @param var1_formula Character, formula for first variable (e.g., "~ Region")
#' @param var2_formula Character, formula for second variable (e.g., "~ Waveform_Type")
#' @return List with varpart result and sequential PERMANOVA results
variance_partition_permanova <- function(beta_result, var1_formula, var2_formula) {

  dist_mat <- beta_result$distance
  meta <- beta_result$metadata

  # Method 1: varpart
  varpart_result <- varpart(dist_mat,
                            as.formula(var1_formula),
                            as.formula(var2_formula),
                            data = meta)

  # Method 2: Sequential PERMANOVA (variable 1 first)
  formula_seq1 <- paste("dist_mat", var1_formula, "+",
                       gsub("~\\s*", "", var2_formula))
  perm_seq1 <- adonis2(as.formula(formula_seq1),
                      data = meta, permutations = 999, by = "terms")

  # Method 3: Sequential PERMANOVA (variable 2 first)
  formula_seq2 <- paste("dist_mat", var2_formula, "+",
                       gsub("~\\s*", "", var1_formula))
  perm_seq2 <- adonis2(as.formula(formula_seq2),
                      data = meta, permutations = 999, by = "terms")

  # Method 4: Marginal effects
  formula_marginal <- paste("dist_mat", var1_formula, "+",
                           gsub("~\\s*", "", var2_formula))
  perm_marginal <- adonis2(as.formula(formula_marginal),
                          data = meta, permutations = 999, by = "margin")

  return(list(
    varpart = varpart_result,
    sequential_var1_first = perm_seq1,
    sequential_var2_first = perm_seq2,
    marginal = perm_marginal
  ))
}


#' Calculate diet overlap using Schoener and Pianka indices
#'
#' @param ps phyloseq object
#' @param group_var Character, grouping variable (e.g., "Species")
#' @return List with Schoener and Pianka matrices
calculate_diet_overlap <- function(ps, group_var) {

  # Convert to relative abundance
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps, function(x) x / sum(x))

  # Get OTU table (taxa as rows, samples as columns)
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps_rel))
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_rel)) {
    otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
  }

  # Get metadata
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_rel))

  # Calculate mean abundance per group
  groups <- unique(meta[[group_var]])
  mean_abundances <- matrix(0, nrow = ntaxa(ps_rel), ncol = length(groups))
  rownames(mean_abundances) <- taxa_names(ps_rel)
  colnames(mean_abundances) <- groups

  for (g in groups) {
    samples_in_group <- rownames(meta)[meta[[group_var]] == g]
    if (length(samples_in_group) == 1) {
      mean_abundances[, g] <- otu_mat[, samples_in_group]
    } else {
      mean_abundances[, g] <- rowMeans(otu_mat[, samples_in_group, drop = FALSE])
    }
  }

  # Transpose for spaa package (expects species as columns, sites/groups as rows)
  mean_abundances_t <- t(mean_abundances)

  # Calculate overlap indices
  schoener <- niche.overlap(mean_abundances_t, method = "schoener")
  pianka <- niche.overlap(mean_abundances_t, method = "pianka")

  return(list(
    schoener = schoener,
    pianka = pianka,
    mean_abundances = mean_abundances
  ))
}
```

---

# PRIMER SET CONCORDANCE ANALYSIS

## Overview

This section quantifies concordance between PS1 and PS2 on shared samples to address concerns about primer effects in metabarcoding. Esnaola et al. (2018) found primer choice explained R² = 0.353 of variation in fish diet - nearly twice the study site effect. We test whether our primers show similar effects when applied to identical biological samples.

```{r primer-concordance-setup}
cat("\n=== PRIMER SET CONCORDANCE ANALYSIS ===\n\n")

# Subset to shared samples only (fair comparison)
ps1_shared <- subset_samples(ps1, Sample_Name %in% shared_samples)
ps2_shared <- subset_samples(ps2, Sample_Name %in% shared_samples)

# Remove absent taxa
ps1_shared <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps1_shared) > 0, ps1_shared)
ps2_shared <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps2_shared) > 0, ps2_shared)

cat(sprintf("Shared samples: n = %d\n", length(shared_samples)))
cat(sprintf("PS1 taxa after pruning: %d\n", ntaxa(ps1_shared)))
cat(sprintf("PS2 taxa after pruning: %d\n", ntaxa(ps2_shared)))
```

```{r primer-beta-diversity}
cat("\n=== Beta Diversity Analysis (Species Effect) ===\n\n")

# PS1
beta1_shared <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps1_shared, "~ Species")
species_r2_ps1 <- beta1_shared$permanova$R2[1]
species_p_ps1 <- beta1_shared$permanova$`Pr(>F)`[1]

cat(sprintf("PS1 - Species R²: %.3f (p = %s)\n",
            species_r2_ps1, format.pval(species_p_ps1, digits = 3)))

# PS2
beta2_shared <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps2_shared, "~ Species")
species_r2_ps2 <- beta2_shared$permanova$R2[1]
species_p_ps2 <- beta2_shared$permanova$`Pr(>F)`[1]

cat(sprintf("PS2 - Species R²: %.3f (p = %s)\n",
            species_r2_ps2, format.pval(species_p_ps2, digits = 3)))

mean_r2 <- mean(c(species_r2_ps1, species_r2_ps2))
cat(sprintf("\nMean Species R²: %.3f\n", mean_r2))
cat(sprintf("Esnaola primer effect threshold: 0.353\n"))
```

```{r primer-concordance-tests}
cat("\n=== Concordance Tests ===\n\n")

# Ensure samples match by Sample_Name (not SampleID)
# Convert distance matrices to matrices with Sample_Name labels
dist1_mat <- as.matrix(beta1_shared$distance)
dist2_mat <- as.matrix(beta2_shared$distance)

# Relabel with Sample_Names from metadata
rownames(dist1_mat) <- beta1_shared$metadata$Sample_Name
colnames(dist1_mat) <- beta1_shared$metadata$Sample_Name
rownames(dist2_mat) <- beta2_shared$metadata$Sample_Name
colnames(dist2_mat) <- beta2_shared$metadata$Sample_Name

# Find truly shared samples and subset (automatically aligned by name)
shared_check <- intersect(beta1_shared$metadata$Sample_Name,
                          beta2_shared$metadata$Sample_Name)
cat(sprintf("Samples matched by Sample_Name: %d\n", length(shared_check)))

dist1_aligned <- as.dist(dist1_mat[shared_check, shared_check])
dist2_aligned <- as.dist(dist2_mat[shared_check, shared_check])

# Mantel test - correlation between distance matrices
mantel_result <- run_mantel_test(dist1_aligned, dist2_aligned)
cat(sprintf("Mantel correlation: r = %.3f (p = %s)\n",
            mantel_result$statistic,
            format.pval(mantel_result$signif, digits = 3)))

# For Procrustes, also need to align PCoA coordinates by Sample_Name
pcoa1_mat <- beta1_shared$pcoa$points
pcoa2_mat <- beta2_shared$pcoa$points
rownames(pcoa1_mat) <- beta1_shared$metadata$Sample_Name
rownames(pcoa2_mat) <- beta2_shared$metadata$Sample_Name

# Subset and reorder to match
pcoa1_aligned <- pcoa1_mat[shared_check, ]
pcoa2_aligned <- pcoa2_mat[shared_check, ]

# Procrustes analysis - ordination congruence
library(vegan)
pro <- procrustes(pcoa1_aligned, pcoa2_aligned, symmetric = TRUE)
pro_test <- protest(pcoa1_aligned, pcoa2_aligned, permutations = 999)

# pro_test$t0 IS the correlation, not m²
pro_cor <- pro_test$t0  # correlation directly from protest()
pro_m2 <- 1 - pro_test$t0^2  # calculate m² from correlation

cat(sprintf("Procrustes correlation: %.3f (higher = better concordance, p = %s)\n",
            pro_cor, format.pval(pro_test$signif, digits = 3)))
cat(sprintf("Procrustes m²: %.4f (lower = better fit)\n",
            pro_m2))

cat("\n=== Primer Set Effect Size (PERMANOVA) ===\n\n")

# Create merged dataset to test Primer_Set effect
# Merge OTU tables (union of all taxa, filling zeros where absent)
otu1 <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps1_shared))
otu2 <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps2_shared))

# Get union of all taxa
all_taxa <- union(rownames(otu1), rownames(otu2))

# Create full matrices with zeros for missing taxa
otu1_full <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_taxa), ncol = ncol(otu1))
rownames(otu1_full) <- all_taxa
colnames(otu1_full) <- colnames(otu1)
otu1_full[rownames(otu1), ] <- otu1

otu2_full <- matrix(0, nrow = length(all_taxa), ncol = ncol(otu2))
rownames(otu2_full) <- all_taxa
colnames(otu2_full) <- colnames(otu2)
otu2_full[rownames(otu2), ] <- otu2

# Relabel samples with Sample_Name and add PS suffix for uniqueness
meta1 <- beta1_shared$metadata[match(colnames(otu1), rownames(beta1_shared$metadata)), ]
meta2 <- beta2_shared$metadata[match(colnames(otu2), rownames(beta2_shared$metadata)), ]

colnames(otu1_full) <- paste0(meta1$Sample_Name, "_PS1")
colnames(otu2_full) <- paste0(meta2$Sample_Name, "_PS2")

# Combine OTU tables
otu_combined <- cbind(otu1_full, otu2_full)

# Create combined metadata
meta1$SampleID_new <- paste0(meta1$Sample_Name, "_PS1")
meta1$Primer_Set <- "PS1"
meta2$SampleID_new <- paste0(meta2$Sample_Name, "_PS2")
meta2$Primer_Set <- "PS2"

meta_combined <- rbind(meta1, meta2)
rownames(meta_combined) <- meta_combined$SampleID_new

# Transpose for vegan (samples as rows)
otu_combined_t <- t(otu_combined)

# Calculate distance and run PERMANOVA
dist_combined <- vegdist(otu_combined_t, method = "bray")

# Test Primer_Set effect (controlling for Species)
perm_primer <- adonis2(dist_combined ~ Primer_Set + Species,
                       data = meta_combined,
                       permutations = 999)

primer_r2 <- perm_primer$R2[1]
primer_p <- perm_primer$`Pr(>F)`[1]

cat(sprintf("Unstratified PERMANOVA:\n"))
cat(sprintf("Primer_Set R²: %.3f (p = %s)\n",
            primer_r2, format.pval(primer_p, digits = 3)))
cat(sprintf("Esnaola et al. primer effect: R² = 0.353\n"))
cat(sprintf("Our primer effect: %.1f%% of Esnaola's\n\n",
            (primer_r2 / 0.353) * 100))

# Stratified PERMANOVA - accounts for paired samples (same fish, both primers)
perm_primer_strata <- adonis2(dist_combined ~ Primer_Set,
                               data = meta_combined,
                               strata = meta_combined$Sample_Name,
                               permutations = 999)

primer_r2_strata <- perm_primer_strata$R2[1]
primer_p_strata <- perm_primer_strata$`Pr(>F)`[1]

cat(sprintf("Stratified by Sample (paired design):\n"))
cat(sprintf("Primer_Set R²: %.3f (p = %s)\n",
            primer_r2_strata, format.pval(primer_p_strata, digits = 3)))
cat(sprintf("Esnaola comparison: %.1f%% of their primer effect\n",
            (primer_r2_strata / 0.353) * 100))
```

```{r primer-pcoa-plots, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
# Use aligned coordinates that match by Sample_Name
pcoa1_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pcoa1_aligned[, 1],
  PC2 = pcoa1_aligned[, 2],
  Sample_Name = rownames(pcoa1_aligned),
  Primer_Set = "PS1"
)

pcoa2_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pcoa2_aligned[, 1],
  PC2 = pcoa2_aligned[, 2],
  Sample_Name = rownames(pcoa2_aligned),
  Primer_Set = "PS2"
)

# Add Species labels from metadata
meta_shared <- beta1_shared$metadata %>%
  filter(Sample_Name %in% shared_check) %>%
  select(Sample_Name, Species)

pcoa1_df <- merge(pcoa1_df, meta_shared, by = "Sample_Name")
pcoa2_df <- merge(pcoa2_df, meta_shared, by = "Sample_Name")

pcoa_combined <- rbind(pcoa1_df, pcoa2_df)

# Create faceted plot
ggplot(pcoa_combined, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Species)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Primer_Set) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Beta Diversity: Comparing Primer Sets on Shared Samples",
       subtitle = sprintf("Mantel r = %.3f | PS1 R² = %.3f | PS2 R² = %.3f",
                          mantel_result$statistic, species_r2_ps1, species_r2_ps2)) +
  coord_fixed()
```

```{r primer-procrustes-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=8}
# Extract transformed coordinates (pro object already uses aligned matrices)
coords_ps1 <- data.frame(pro$X)
coords_ps2 <- data.frame(pro$Yrot)

# Create plot dataframe with proper column names
plot_df <- data.frame(
  x1 = coords_ps1[, 1],
  y1 = coords_ps1[, 2],
  x2 = coords_ps2[, 1],
  y2 = coords_ps2[, 2]
)

ggplot(plot_df) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = x1, y = y1, xend = x2, yend = y2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.1, "cm")),
               alpha = 0.3, color = "gray50") +
  geom_point(aes(x = x1, y = y1),
             color = "#2C7BB6", size = 3, alpha = 0.6, shape = 16) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x2, y = y2),
             color = "#D7191C", size = 3, alpha = 0.6, shape = 17) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Procrustes Superimposition: PS1 (circles) vs PS2 (triangles)",
       subtitle = sprintf(
         "Correlation = %.3f (higher = better) | m² = %.4f (lower = better) | p = %.3f",
         pro_cor, pro_m2, pro_test$signif)) +
  coord_fixed()
```

```{r primer-effect-comparison, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
r2_comparison <- data.frame(
  Factor = c("Species (PS1)", "Species (PS2)",
             "This Study: Primer Effect (unstratified)",
             "This Study: Primer Effect (stratified)",
             "Esnaola: Primer Effect", "Esnaola: Study Site"),
  R2 = c(species_r2_ps1, species_r2_ps2, primer_r2, primer_r2_strata,
         0.353, 0.184),
  Study = c("This Study", "This Study", "This Study", "This Study",
            "Esnaola 2018", "Esnaola 2018"),
  Type = c("Biological", "Biological", "Technical", "Technical",
           "Technical", "Biological")
)

ggplot(r2_comparison, aes(x = reorder(Factor, R2), y = R2, fill = Type)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.353, linetype = "dashed", color = "red",
             linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 0.37, label = "Esnaola primer threshold",
           size = 3, color = "red") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Effect Sizes: Biological vs Technical Variation",
       subtitle = sprintf("Our primer effect (R²=%.3f) is %.0f%% of Esnaola's",
                          primer_r2, (primer_r2/0.353)*100),
       x = "", y = "R² (Proportion of Variation Explained)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Biological" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Technical" = "#D7191C"))
```

```{r primer-stats-table}
primer_stats <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Shared Samples (n)",
    "PS1: Species R²",
    "PS1: Species p-value",
    "PS2: Species R²",
    "PS2: Species p-value",
    "Mean Species R²",
    "Primer Effect R² (unstratified)",
    "Primer Effect p-value (unstratified)",
    "Primer Effect R² (stratified)",
    "Primer Effect p-value (stratified)",
    "Mantel correlation (r)",
    "Mantel p-value",
    "Procrustes correlation (high = good)",
    "Procrustes m² (low = good)",
    "Procrustes p-value"
  ),
  Value = c(
    length(shared_samples),
    round(species_r2_ps1, 3),
    format.pval(species_p_ps1, digits = 3),
    round(species_r2_ps2, 3),
    format.pval(species_p_ps2, digits = 3),
    round(mean(c(species_r2_ps1, species_r2_ps2)), 3),
    round(primer_r2, 3),
    format.pval(primer_p, digits = 3),
    round(primer_r2_strata, 3),
    format.pval(primer_p_strata, digits = 3),
    round(mantel_result$statistic, 3),
    format.pval(mantel_result$signif, digits = 3),
    round(pro_cor, 3),
    round(pro_m2, 4),
    format.pval(pro_test$signif, digits = 3)
  )
)

kable(primer_stats, caption = "Primer Set Concordance Statistics") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"))
```

## Compositional Differences vs. Variation Explained

### Conceptual Framework

Before interpreting the results, it's critical to distinguish between two related but
distinct concepts:

1. **Compositional differences (univariate)**: Taxon-specific absolute shifts in
   relative abundance between primer sets (e.g., "Hydroptilidae is 321× more abundant
   in PS1 than PS2"). Measured as fold-change ratios for individual taxa.

2. **Compositional variation explained (multivariate)**: The proportion of
   sample-to-sample variation in community composition attributable to primer choice
   (R² from PERMANOVA). Measures how much primers *differentially* affect samples.

**Why these can diverge:** If all samples experience the same absolute shift
(e.g., all samples show 10× more Hydroptilidae in PS1), the fold-change is huge
(10×) but the R² is low because the primer doesn't change the *relative differences*
between samples—the ecological structure (which samples are similar/different) is
preserved. The primers shift all samples in parallel, maintaining beta diversity
patterns.

Conversely, high R² with moderate fold-changes indicates that primers *differentially*
affect samples (Sample A shifts one way, Sample B shifts another), disrupting the
ecological signal.

```{r taxon-fold-changes}
cat("\n=== Taxon-Specific Compositional Differences ===\n\n")

# Calculate mean relative abundance by family for each primer set
# Transform to relative abundance
ps1_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps1_shared, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)
ps2_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps2_shared, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)

# Aggregate by family and calculate mean across all samples
ps1_family <- tax_glom(ps1_rel, taxrank = "Family", NArm = FALSE)
ps2_family <- tax_glom(ps2_rel, taxrank = "Family", NArm = FALSE)

# Get mean abundance per family
ps1_means <- apply(otu_table(ps1_family), 1, mean)
ps2_means <- apply(otu_table(ps2_family), 1, mean)

# Get family names
ps1_tax <- tax_table(ps1_family)[, "Family"]
ps2_tax <- tax_table(ps2_family)[, "Family"]

# Create dataframes
ps1_df <- data.frame(
  Family = as.character(ps1_tax),
  PS1_mean_pct = ps1_means,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

ps2_df <- data.frame(
  Family = as.character(ps2_tax),
  PS2_mean_pct = ps2_means,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Merge and calculate fold-changes
fc_data <- merge(ps1_df, ps2_df, by = "Family", all = TRUE)
fc_data$PS1_mean_pct[is.na(fc_data$PS1_mean_pct)] <- 0.001  # pseudocount
fc_data$PS2_mean_pct[is.na(fc_data$PS2_mean_pct)] <- 0.001  # pseudocount

fc_data$fold_change <- fc_data$PS1_mean_pct / fc_data$PS2_mean_pct
fc_data$log2_fc <- log2(fc_data$fold_change)
fc_data$abs_log2_fc <- abs(fc_data$log2_fc)

# Sort by absolute fold-change
fc_data <- fc_data[order(-fc_data$abs_log2_fc), ]

# Get top 10 families by abundance
top_families <- fc_data %>%
  mutate(max_abundance = pmax(PS1_mean_pct, PS2_mean_pct)) %>%
  arrange(desc(max_abundance)) %>%
  head(10)

cat(sprintf("Maximum fold-change observed: %.1f× (%s)\n",
            fc_data$fold_change[1], fc_data$Family[1]))
cat(sprintf("Number of families with >10× difference: %d\n",
            sum(fc_data$fold_change > 10 | fc_data$fold_change < 0.1)))
cat(sprintf("\n#### Top 10 most abundant families (fold-change = PS1/PS2):\n\n"))
kable(top_families[, c("Family", "PS1_mean_pct", "PS2_mean_pct",
                             "fold_change")],
            digits = c(0, 2, 2, 1),
            col.names = c("Family", "PS1 Mean %", "PS2 Mean %",
                          "Fold-Change"),
            caption = "Compositional Differences: Top 10 Families",
            booktabs = TRUE) %>%
      kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position"),
                    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
                    full_width = FALSE, font_size = 9)
```

```{r fold-change-plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Create visualization showing fold-changes for top families
top_families_plot <- top_families %>%
  mutate(
    Direction = ifelse(fold_change > 1, "Higher in PS1", "Higher in PS2"),
    Family_label = paste0(Family, " (", round(fold_change, 1), "×)")
  )

ggplot(top_families_plot,
       aes(x = reorder(Family, abs_log2_fc), y = log2_fc, fill = Direction)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50",
             alpha = 0.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Taxon-Specific Compositional Differences Between Primer Sets",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "Top 10 families by abundance | Max fold-change: %.1f× | Primer R² = %.3f",
      fc_data$fold_change[1], primer_r2_strata
    ),
    x = "Family",
    y = "Log2 Fold-Change (PS1/PS2)",
    caption = "Dashed lines: 2× fold-change threshold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Higher in PS1" = "#2C7BB6",
                               "Higher in PS2" = "#D7191C")) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r compositional-reconciliation, results='asis'}
cat("\n### Reconciling Large Fold-Changes with Low R²\n\n")

max_fc <- fc_data$fold_change[1]
max_fc_family <- fc_data$Family[1]

cat(sprintf("We observe substantial taxon-specific compositional differences between
primer sets, with a maximum fold-change of %.1f× for %s. Yet our stratified
PERMANOVA shows primer choice explains only R² = %.3f (%.1f%%) of sample-to-sample
variation.\n\n", max_fc, max_fc_family, primer_r2_strata,
primer_r2_strata * 100))

if (primer_r2_strata < 0.15) {
  cat("This apparent paradox—large univariate shifts but low multivariate R²—reveals
that the primers are shifting community composition in a **consistent, parallel
manner across all samples**. When all fish show similar directional shifts
(e.g., all samples gain Hydroptilidae and lose Chironomidae in the same proportions),
the absolute compositional changes are large, but the *relative ecological
relationships between samples* are preserved.\n\n")

  cat("This is actually **good news for cross-primer comparisons**: it means that
while the primers detect different absolute abundances, they maintain the
ecological structure (which species are similar vs. different). The biological
signal—species-level dietary differences—is robust to primer choice because the
primer bias affects all samples uniformly.\n\n")

  cat(sprintf("In contrast, Esnaola et al. (2018) found R² = 0.353 for primer
effects, suggesting their primers *differentially* affected samples in ways that
disrupted ecological patterns. Our low R² (%.3f) with high concordance
(Mantel r = %.3f, Procrustes r = %.3f) demonstrates primer compatibility despite
absolute compositional shifts.\n\n", primer_r2_strata, mantel_result$statistic,
pro_cor))
} else {
  cat("The combination of large fold-changes with moderate-to-high R² suggests that
primers are *differentially* affecting samples (some samples shift more than others),
which can disrupt ecological patterns. This indicates primer choice is a more
critical consideration for interpreting community structure.\n\n")
}
```

## Interpretation

```{r primer-interpretation, results='asis'}
# Generate interpretation based on actual results
cat("\n### Biological Signal Strength\n\n")

if (species_r2_ps1 > 0.353 && species_r2_ps2 > 0.353) {
  cat(sprintf("Both primer sets demonstrate strong biological signal: PS1 (R² = %.3f) and PS2 (R² = %.3f) both exceed the primer effect threshold (R² = 0.353) documented by Esnaola et al. (2018). This indicates that species-level dietary variation dominates over any potential technical variation introduced by primer choice.\n\n", species_r2_ps1, species_r2_ps2))
} else if (mean_r2 > 0.353) {
  cat(sprintf("On average, biological variation (mean R² = %.3f) exceeds the technical variation threshold from Esnaola et al. (R² = 0.353), though individual primer sets show variable strength (PS1: %.3f, PS2: %.3f).\n\n", mean_r2, species_r2_ps1, species_r2_ps2))
} else {
  cat(sprintf("Both primer sets show biological signal below Esnaola's primer effect threshold (PS1: %.3f, PS2: %.3f), suggesting moderate species-level dietary differentiation at this site.\n\n", species_r2_ps1, species_r2_ps2))
}

cat("### Primer Concordance\n\n")

# Determine concordance levels
mantel_level <- ifelse(mantel_result$statistic > 0.6, "strong",
                      ifelse(mantel_result$statistic > 0.4, "moderate", "weak"))
# For Procrustes: LOW m² = GOOD fit, HIGH correlation = GOOD concordance
procrustes_level <- ifelse(pro_cor > 0.8, "strong",
                          ifelse(pro_cor > 0.6, "moderate", "weak"))

cat(sprintf("The two primer sets show %s concordance in both distance-based (Mantel r = %.3f, p %s) and ordination-based (Procrustes correlation = %.3f, m² = %.4f, p %s) tests. ",
            mantel_level, mantel_result$statistic,
            format.pval(mantel_result$signif, digits = 3),
            pro_cor, pro_m2,
            format.pval(pro_test$signif, digits = 3)))

# Note: LOW m² means GOOD fit (0 = perfect superimposition)
if (mantel_result$statistic > 0.6 && pro_cor > 0.8) {
  cat("Both metrics indicate highly consistent dietary patterns despite sequencing different molecular markers, demonstrating that our results are robust to primer choice.\n\n")
} else if (mantel_result$statistic > 0.4 || pro_cor > 0.6) {
  cat("The primers detect reasonably consistent patterns, though some technical variation is evident.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("Concordance is limited, suggesting potential primer-specific biases that should be considered when interpreting results.\n\n")
}

cat("### Primer Effect Size\n\n")

cat(sprintf("Direct PERMANOVA testing shows that primer choice explains R² = %.3f (%.1f%%) of community variation (unstratified) and R² = %.3f (%.1f%%) when accounting for the paired design (stratified by sample). ",
            primer_r2, primer_r2 * 100, primer_r2_strata, primer_r2_strata * 100))

# Use stratified result for primary interpretation (accounts for paired design)
primer_effect_primary <- primer_r2_strata

if (primer_effect_primary < 0.353) {
  cat(sprintf("The stratified analysis (R² = %.3f) represents %.0f%% of the primer effect observed by Esnaola et al. (R² = 0.353), suggesting our primers show substantially less bias than the universal COI primers tested in their Mediterranean fish diet study. ",
              primer_effect_primary, (primer_effect_primary / 0.353) * 100))

  if (primer_effect_primary < mean_r2) {
    cat(sprintf("Importantly, our primer effect (R² = %.3f) is smaller than the species effect in both primer sets (mean R² = %.3f), demonstrating that biological signal dominates over technical variation.\n\n",
                primer_effect_primary, mean_r2))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("However, this primer effect (R² = %.3f) is comparable to or exceeds the species effect (mean R² = %.3f), suggesting substantial technical variation that should be considered in interpretation.\n\n",
                primer_effect_primary, mean_r2))
  }
} else {
  cat(sprintf("This matches or exceeds Esnaola's primer effect (R² = 0.353), indicating substantial technical variation comparable to their study.\n\n"))
}

cat("### Contrast to Esnaola et al. (2018)\n\n")
cat(sprintf("Esnaola et al. reported that primer choice explained 35.3%% of variation (nearly twice their study site effect of 18.4%%). Our paired-sample design, where both primers are applied to identical biological material, yields a primer effect of R² = %.3f (%.1f%%) in the stratified analysis that accounts for paired samples. ",
            primer_r2_strata, primer_r2_strata * 100))

if (primer_r2_strata < 0.353 && mantel_result$statistic > 0.5) {
  cat(sprintf("The combination of lower primer effect size (%.1f%% of Esnaola's) and strong concordance (Mantel r = %.3f) suggests that our primers are more compatible than those tested by Esnaola, possibly due to better taxonomic coverage in our study system or reduced primer bias. ",
      (primer_r2_strata / 0.353) * 100, mantel_result$statistic))
  cat("Additionally, their large primer effect may have been partially confounded by uncontrolled differences in sample composition or environmental conditions between primer sets, which our paired design explicitly controls for.\n\n")
} else if (primer_r2_strata >= 0.353) {
  cat(sprintf("Like Esnaola et al., we observe substantial primer-related variation, reinforcing the importance of primer choice in metabarcoding studies. This primer effect (R² = %.3f) is comparable to Esnaola's finding, suggesting it may be an inherent challenge in multi-primer COI metabarcoding rather than a study-specific artifact.\n\n",
              primer_r2_strata))
} else {
  cat("While our primer effect is reduced compared to Esnaola's, the moderate concordance suggests that primer choice remains an important consideration in metabarcoding study design and interpretation.\n\n")
}
```

---

# LEVEL 1: Mormyrid Community at Balé Creek

**Biological context:** 9 mormyrid species, 35 individuals, single site. This level controls for geography and examines dietary differences among sympatric species.

## 1.1 Prey Composition Summary

Family-level prey composition across the 9 Balé Creek mormyrid species.

```{r level1-prey-composition}
cat("=== LEVEL 1.1: PREY COMPOSITION (FAMILY LEVEL) ===\n\n")

# Aggregate to family level
ps1_bale_family <- aggregate_to_family(ps1_bale)
ps2_bale_family <- aggregate_to_family(ps2_bale)

cat("Family-level aggregation:\n")
cat("  PS1:", ntaxa(ps1_bale_family), "families\n")
cat("  PS2:", ntaxa(ps2_bale_family), "families\n")

# Calculate summary statistics (top families)
# Convert to relative abundance and get mean ± SD per species
ps1_bale_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps1_bale_family, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)
ps2_bale_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps2_bale_family, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)

# Melt and summarize
calc_family_summary <- function(ps_rel) {
  ps_melt <- psmelt(ps_rel)
  ps_melt$Family_clean <- sapply(ps_melt$Family, clean_family_name)

  summary_table <- ps_melt %>%
    group_by(Family_clean) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_pct = mean(Abundance),
      sd_pct = sd(Abundance),
      n_samples = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_pct)) %>%
    mutate(mean_sd = paste0(round(mean_pct, 2), " ± ", round(sd_pct, 2)))

  return(summary_table)
}

summary_ps1 <- calc_family_summary(ps1_bale_rel)
summary_ps2 <- calc_family_summary(ps2_bale_rel)

cat("\nTop 10 prey families (Primer Set 1):\n")
print(head(summary_ps1, 10))

cat("\nTop 10 prey families (Primer Set 2):\n")
print(head(summary_ps2, 10))

# Identify top families across BOTH datasets for unified legend
combined_summary <- bind_rows(
  summary_ps1 %>% mutate(Dataset = "PS1"),
  summary_ps2 %>% mutate(Dataset = "PS2")
) %>%
  group_by(Family_clean) %>%
  summarise(Mean_Prop = mean(mean_pct), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_Prop)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

top_families_unified <- combined_summary$Family_clean

# Ensure all top families have colors assigned in global palette
missing_families <- setdiff(top_families_unified, names(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS))
if (length(missing_families) > 0) {
  cat("Adding colors for", length(missing_families), "families not in global palette:\n")
  cat("  ", paste(missing_families, collapse = ", "), "\n")

  # Get additional colors from the same palette used in create_global_family_palette
  distinct_colors <- c(
    "#4477AA", "#EE6677", "#228833", "#CCBB44", "#66CCEE", "#AA3377", "#BBBBBB",
    "#EE3377", "#009988", "#CC3311", "#EE7733", "#0077BB", "#33BBEE", "#EE3377",
    "#CC6677", "#882255", "#AA4499", "#117733", "#332288", "#44AA99", "#999933",
    "#DDCC77", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#AA4466", "#888888", "#771122", "#DD7788"
  )

  # Find colors not yet used
  used_colors <- GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS[names(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS) != "Other"]
  available_colors <- setdiff(distinct_colors, used_colors)

  # Add colors for missing families
  new_colors <- setNames(
    available_colors[1:length(missing_families)],
    missing_families
  )
  GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS <- c(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS, new_colors)
}

# Create modified plot function that uses pre-specified families
plot_composition_unified <- function(ps, group_var, families_to_show, title = "") {
  ps_melt <- psmelt(ps)
  ps_melt$Family_clean <- sapply(ps_melt$Family, clean_family_name)

  ps_agg <- ps_melt %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]], Family_clean) %>%
    summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

  ps_agg <- ps_agg %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]]) %>%
    mutate(RelAbund = Abundance / sum(Abundance)) %>%
    ungroup()

  # Use provided families
  ps_final <- ps_agg %>%
    mutate(Family_display = ifelse(Family_clean %in% families_to_show,
                                   Family_clean, "Other")) %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]], Family_display) %>%
    summarise(RelAbund = sum(RelAbund), .groups = "drop")

  # Consistent factor levels across all plots
  all_levels <- c(families_to_show, "Other")
  ps_final$Family_display <- factor(ps_final$Family_display,
                                     levels = all_levels)

  p <- ggplot(ps_final, aes(x = .data[[group_var]], y = RelAbund,
                            fill = Family_display)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS,
                      breaks = all_levels,
                      name = "Prey Family",
                      na.value = "#808080",
                      drop = FALSE) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = title, x = group_var, y = "Relative Abundance") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)

  return(p)
}

# Create stacked bar plots with unified family list
p1 <- plot_composition_unified(ps1_bale_family, group_var = "sample_Species",
                               families_to_show = top_families_unified,
                               title = "Prey Composition - Balé Creek (PS1)")

p2 <- plot_composition_unified(ps2_bale_family, group_var = "sample_Species",
                               families_to_show = top_families_unified,
                               title = "Prey Composition - Balé Creek (PS2)")

# Display side by side with unified legend
# Manually create unified legend since patchwork guides="collect" isn't working
library(cowplot)

# Remove legends from both plots
p1_no_legend <- p1 + theme(legend.position = "none")
p2_no_legend <- p2 + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Create a dummy plot with ALL families for legend extraction
# Combine data from both plots to ensure all families appear
dummy_data <- data.frame(
  x = rep("A", length(top_families_unified) + 1),
  y = rep(1, length(top_families_unified) + 1),
  Family = factor(c(top_families_unified, "Other"),
                  levels = c(top_families_unified, "Other"))
)

p_dummy <- ggplot(dummy_data, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = Family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS,
                    breaks = c(top_families_unified, "Other"),
                    name = "Prey Family",
                    drop = FALSE) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm")) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 5, byrow = TRUE, title = "Prey Family"))

# Extract legend from dummy plot (has all families)
legend <- get_legend(p_dummy)

# Combine plots and legend
plot_grid(
  plot_grid(p1_no_legend, p2_no_legend, ncol = 2),
  legend,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.15)
)
```

Top prey families table:

```{r level1-top-families-table}
# Combine top families from both primer sets
top_families_combined <- full_join(
  summary_ps1 %>% select(Family_clean, mean_pct, sd_pct) %>% slice_head(n = 15),
  summary_ps2 %>% select(Family_clean, mean_pct, sd_pct) %>% slice_head(n = 15),
  by = "Family_clean",
  suffix = c("_PS1", "_PS2")
) %>%
  arrange(desc(pmax(mean_pct_PS1, mean_pct_PS2, na.rm = TRUE)))

kable(top_families_combined,
      caption = "Top 15 prey families at Balé Creek (mean $\\pm$ SD relative abundance \\%)",
      digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Family", "Mean \\% (PS1)", "SD (PS1)", "Mean \\% (PS2)", "SD (PS2)"),
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## 1.2 Alpha Diversity Among Species

Shannon diversity analysis across Balé Creek species with cross-primer validation.

```{r level1-alpha-diversity, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
cat("=== LEVEL 1.2: ALPHA DIVERSITY AMONG SPECIES ===\n\n")

# Analyze alpha diversity for both primer sets
alpha1_bale <- analyze_alpha_diversity(ps1_bale, "Species")
alpha2_bale <- analyze_alpha_diversity(ps2_bale, "Species")

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 1 - Balé Creek Shannon Diversity ===\n")
cat("Test type:", alpha1_bale$test_type, "\n")
cat("Shapiro-Wilk p-value:", round(alpha1_bale$shapiro_p, 4), "\n")
if (alpha1_bale$test_type == "Kruskal-Wallis") {
  print(alpha1_bale$test)
} else {
  print(summary(alpha1_bale$test))
}

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 2 - Balé Creek Shannon Diversity ===\n")
cat("Test type:", alpha2_bale$test_type, "\n")
cat("Shapiro-Wilk p-value:", round(alpha2_bale$shapiro_p, 4), "\n")
if (alpha2_bale$test_type == "Kruskal-Wallis") {
  print(alpha2_bale$test)
} else {
  print(summary(alpha2_bale$test))
}

# Comparative plot
p_bale_alpha_comp <- plot_comparative_shannon(alpha1_bale$diversity,
                                              alpha2_bale$diversity,
                                              "Species",
                                              "Balé Creek Species Diet Diversity Comparison")
print(p_bale_alpha_comp)
```

### Cross-Primer Correlation for Alpha Diversity

```{r level1-alpha-correlation, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Correlate Shannon values for shared samples
cor_bale_alpha <- correlate_shared_samples(alpha1_bale$diversity,
                                          alpha2_bale$diversity)

cat("\n=== BALÉ CREEK ALPHA DIVERSITY CORRELATION ===\n")
cat("Shared samples:", cor_bale_alpha$n_shared, "\n")

if (!is.null(cor_bale_alpha$cor_test)) {
  cat("Pearson r:", round(cor_bale_alpha$cor_test$estimate, 3), "\n")
  cat("95% CI: [", round(cor_bale_alpha$cor_test$conf.int[1], 3), ",",
      round(cor_bale_alpha$cor_test$conf.int[2], 3), "]\n")
  cat("P-value:", format.pval(cor_bale_alpha$cor_test$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")

  # Scatterplot - get shared samples
  shared_ids <- intersect(alpha1_bale$diversity$Sample_Name,
                          alpha2_bale$diversity$Sample_Name)

  # Filter to shared samples and match order
  div1_shared <- alpha1_bale$diversity %>%
    filter(Sample_Name %in% shared_ids) %>%
    arrange(Sample_Name)
  div2_shared <- alpha2_bale$diversity %>%
    filter(Sample_Name %in% shared_ids) %>%
    arrange(Sample_Name)

  cor_df <- data.frame(
    PS1_Shannon = div1_shared$Shannon,
    PS2_Shannon = div2_shared$Shannon
  )

  p_alpha_cor <- ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = PS1_Shannon, y = PS2_Shannon)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6, color = ps1_color) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "gray50") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Balé Creek: Shannon Diversity Correlation",
         subtitle = paste0("r = ", round(cor_bale_alpha$cor_test$estimate, 3),
                          ", p ", format.pval(cor_bale_alpha$cor_test$p.value, digits = 2)),
         x = "Primerset 1 Shannon", y = "Primerset 2 Shannon") +
    coord_fixed()

  print(p_alpha_cor)
} else {
  cat(cor_bale_alpha$message, "\n")
}
```

## 1.3 Beta Diversity Among Species

Bray-Curtis dissimilarity analysis with PERMANOVA testing for dietary differences among species.

```{r level1-beta-diversity, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 1.3: BETA DIVERSITY AMONG SPECIES ===\n\n")

# Filter species with < 3 samples for stable PERMANOVA
cat("=== PRIMERSET 1 FILTERING ===\n")
ps1_bale_filt <- filter_low_n_species(ps1_bale, min_n = 3)

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 2 FILTERING ===\n")
ps2_bale_filt <- filter_low_n_species(ps2_bale, min_n = 3)

# Run beta diversity analyses
beta1_bale <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps1_bale_filt, "~ Species")
beta2_bale <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps2_bale_filt, "~ Species")

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 1 - Balé Creek PERMANOVA ===\n")
print(beta1_bale$permanova)

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 2 - Balé Creek PERMANOVA ===\n")
print(beta2_bale$permanova)

# Faceted PCoA plot
p_bale_pcoa <- plot_faceted_pcoa(beta1_bale, beta2_bale,
                                 color_var = "Species",
                                 title = "Balé Creek Diet Composition by Species")
print(p_bale_pcoa)
```

### Pairwise PERMANOVA Tests

```{r level1-beta-pairwise}
# Pairwise tests if more than 2 species
n_species_ps1 <- length(unique(sample_data(ps1_bale_filt)$Species))
n_species_ps2 <- length(unique(sample_data(ps2_bale_filt)$Species))

cat("\n=== PAIRWISE PERMANOVA TESTS ===\n")

if (n_species_ps1 > 2) {
  cat("\n#### PRIMERSET 1:\n\n")
  pairwise1_bale <- pairwise.adonis2(beta1_bale$distance ~ Species,
                                     data = beta1_bale$metadata)
  # Sanitize row names for LaTeX (replace _ with space)
  pairwise1_df <- as.data.frame(pairwise1_bale)
  rownames(pairwise1_df) <- gsub("_", " vs ", rownames(pairwise1_df))
  # Select only key columns to fit on page
  key_cols <- intersect(colnames(pairwise1_df), c("R2", "F.Model", "Pr(>F)"))
  if (length(key_cols) == 0) {
    # If those columns don't exist, take first 3 numeric columns plus p-value
    key_cols <- colnames(pairwise1_df)[1:min(4, ncol(pairwise1_df))]
  }
  kable(pairwise1_df[, key_cols, drop = FALSE],
              digits = 3,
              booktabs = TRUE,
              caption = "Pairwise PERMANOVA: Primerset 1") %>%
        kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position"),
                      font_size = 9,
                      full_width = FALSE)
}

if (n_species_ps2 > 2) {
  cat("\n#### PRIMERSET 2:\n\n")
  pairwise2_bale <- pairwise.adonis2(beta2_bale$distance ~ Species,
                                     data = beta2_bale$metadata)
  # Sanitize row names for LaTeX (replace _ with space)
  pairwise2_df <- as.data.frame(pairwise2_bale)
  rownames(pairwise2_df) <- gsub("_", " vs ", rownames(pairwise2_df))
  # Select only key columns to fit on page
  key_cols <- intersect(colnames(pairwise2_df), c("R2", "F.Model", "Pr(>F)"))
  if (length(key_cols) == 0) {
    # If those columns don't exist, take first 3 numeric columns plus p-value
    key_cols <- colnames(pairwise2_df)[1:min(4, ncol(pairwise2_df))]
  }
  kable(pairwise2_df[, key_cols, drop = FALSE],
              digits = 3,
              booktabs = TRUE,
              caption = "Pairwise PERMANOVA: Primerset 2") %>%
        kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position"),
                      font_size = 9,
                      full_width = FALSE)
}
```

### Beta Diversity Cross-Validation (Mantel Test)

```{r level1-beta-mantel}
# Find shared Sample_Names between datasets
samples1_bale <- beta1_bale$metadata$Sample_Name
samples2_bale <- beta2_bale$metadata$Sample_Name
shared_bale <- intersect(samples1_bale, samples2_bale)

cat("\n=== BALÉ CREEK BETA DIVERSITY MANTEL TEST ===\n")
cat("Shared Sample_Names:", length(shared_bale), "\n")

if (length(shared_bale) >= 3) {
  # Convert distance matrices to matrices with Sample_Name labels
  dist1_mat <- as.matrix(beta1_bale$distance)
  dist2_mat <- as.matrix(beta2_bale$distance)

  # Relabel with Sample_Names
  rownames(dist1_mat) <- beta1_bale$metadata$Sample_Name
  colnames(dist1_mat) <- beta1_bale$metadata$Sample_Name
  rownames(dist2_mat) <- beta2_bale$metadata$Sample_Name
  colnames(dist2_mat) <- beta2_bale$metadata$Sample_Name

  # Subset to shared Sample_Names (automatically aligned)
  dist1_shared <- as.dist(dist1_mat[shared_bale, shared_bale])
  dist2_shared <- as.dist(dist2_mat[shared_bale, shared_bale])

  # Mantel test
  mantel_bale <- run_mantel_test(dist1_shared, dist2_shared)

  cat("\nMantel statistic (r):", round(mantel_bale$statistic, 3), "\n")
  cat("P-value:", format.pval(mantel_bale$signif, digits = 3), "\n")
  cat("Interpretation:",
      ifelse(mantel_bale$statistic > 0.5, "Strong concordance",
             ifelse(mantel_bale$statistic > 0.3, "Moderate concordance",
                    "Weak concordance")), "\n")
} else {
  cat("Insufficient shared samples for Mantel test.\n")
  mantel_bale <- NULL
}
```

## 1.4 Diet Overlap Indices

Schoener and Pianka diet overlap indices quantify dietary similarity among species. We test whether overlap is higher within *Paramormyrops* species than between genera.

```{r level1-diet-overlap}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 1.4: DIET OVERLAP INDICES ===\n\n")

# Function to calculate diet overlap for a phyloseq object
calculate_diet_overlap_bale <- function(ps_bale, primerset_name) {

  cat("\n=== ", primerset_name, " - Diet Overlap ===\n", sep = "")

  # Get OTU and taxonomy
  otu_mat <- t(as.matrix(otu_table(ps_bale)))
  tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_bale))
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_bale), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  # Create taxonomic label (Order level)
  tax_df$TaxLabel <- ifelse(!is.na(tax_df$Order) & tax_df$Order != "",
                           tax_df$Order,
                           ifelse(!is.na(tax_df$Family) & tax_df$Family != "",
                                 tax_df$Family,
                                 ifelse(!is.na(tax_df$Class) & tax_df$Class != "",
                                       tax_df$Class, "Unclassified")))

  # Aggregate by taxonomic group
  otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_mat)
  otu_tax <- data.frame(row.names = rownames(otu_df))

  for (taxon in unique(tax_df$TaxLabel)) {
    asvs_in_taxon <- rownames(tax_df)[tax_df$TaxLabel == taxon]
    asvs_present <- asvs_in_taxon[asvs_in_taxon %in% colnames(otu_df)]

    if (length(asvs_present) > 0) {
      if (length(asvs_present) == 1) {
        otu_tax[[taxon]] <- otu_df[[asvs_present]]
      } else {
        otu_tax[[taxon]] <- rowSums(otu_df[, asvs_present, drop = FALSE])
      }
    }
  }

  # Aggregate by species
  otu_agg <- aggregate(otu_tax, by = list(Species = meta$Species), FUN = sum)
  rownames(otu_agg) <- otu_agg$Species
  otu_agg <- otu_agg[, -1]

  # Check number of species
  n_species <- nrow(otu_agg)
  cat("Number of species:", n_species, "\n")

  # Need at least 2 species for pairwise overlap
  if (n_species < 2) {
    cat("Warning: Only", n_species, "species found. Cannot calculate pairwise overlap.\n")
    return(list(schoener = NA, pianka = NA, taxa = unique(tax_df$TaxLabel),
                species_list = rownames(otu_agg)))
  }

  # Convert to matrix for proper handling
  otu_mat_agg <- as.matrix(otu_agg)

  # Check for empty rows
  row_sums <- rowSums(otu_mat_agg)
  cat("Row sums range:", min(row_sums), "-", max(row_sums), "\n")

  if (any(row_sums == 0)) {
    cat("Warning: Found", sum(row_sums == 0), "species with zero reads\n")
    otu_mat_agg <- otu_mat_agg[row_sums > 0, , drop = FALSE]
    cat("Removing empty species. New count:", nrow(otu_mat_agg), "\n")

    if (nrow(otu_mat_agg) < 2) {
      cat("Warning: Less than 2 species with data after filtering.\n")
      return(list(schoener = NA, pianka = NA, taxa = unique(tax_df$TaxLabel),
                  species_list = rownames(otu_mat_agg)))
    }
  }

  # Check for empty columns (taxonomic groups with no reads)
  col_sums <- colSums(otu_mat_agg)
  if (any(col_sums == 0)) {
    cat("Warning: Found", sum(col_sums == 0), "taxa with zero reads\n")
    otu_mat_agg <- otu_mat_agg[, col_sums > 0, drop = FALSE]
    cat("Removing empty taxa. New count:", ncol(otu_mat_agg), "\n")
  }

  # Convert to proportions
  otu_prop <- sweep(otu_mat_agg, 1, rowSums(otu_mat_agg), "/")

  # Check for NaN in proportions
  if (any(is.nan(otu_prop))) {
    cat("Warning: NaN values in proportions matrix\n")
  }

  cat("Proportions matrix dimensions:", nrow(otu_prop), "x", ncol(otu_prop), "\n")

  # Calculate overlap indices
  # NOTE: niche.overlap expects species in COLUMNS, so transpose
  schoener <- niche.overlap(t(otu_prop), method = "schoener")
  pianka <- niche.overlap(t(otu_prop), method = "pianka")

  # CRITICAL: niche.overlap returns a dist-like object, convert to matrix
  species_names <- rownames(otu_prop)
  schoener <- as.matrix(schoener)
  pianka <- as.matrix(pianka)

  # Now assign species names as dimnames
  rownames(schoener) <- species_names
  colnames(schoener) <- species_names
  rownames(pianka) <- species_names
  colnames(pianka) <- species_names

  # Check if matrices have valid values
  cat("Schoener matrix dimensions:", nrow(schoener), "x", ncol(schoener), "\n")
  cat("Schoener matrix range:", min(schoener, na.rm = TRUE), "-",
      max(schoener, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

  # Extract lower triangle for pairwise comparisons
  schoener_vals <- schoener[lower.tri(schoener)]
  pianka_vals <- pianka[lower.tri(pianka)]

  cat("Schoener overlap (mean ± SD):",
      round(mean(schoener_vals, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "±",
      round(sd(schoener_vals, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

  cat("Pianka overlap (mean ± SD):",
      round(mean(pianka_vals, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "±",
      round(sd(pianka_vals, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

  return(list(schoener = schoener,
              pianka = pianka,
              taxa = unique(tax_df$TaxLabel),
              species_list = species_names))
}

# Calculate for both primer sets
overlap_ps1 <- calculate_diet_overlap_bale(ps1_bale, "PRIMERSET 1")
overlap_ps2 <- calculate_diet_overlap_bale(ps2_bale, "PRIMERSET 2")

# Taxonomic overlap
shared_taxa <- intersect(overlap_ps1$taxa, overlap_ps2$taxa)
cat("\n=== TAXONOMIC OVERLAP ===\n")
cat("PS1 unique Orders:", length(overlap_ps1$taxa), "\n")
cat("PS2 unique Orders:", length(overlap_ps2$taxa), "\n")
cat("Shared Orders:", length(shared_taxa), "\n")
cat("Jaccard similarity:",
    round(length(shared_taxa) /
          length(union(overlap_ps1$taxa, overlap_ps2$taxa)), 3), "\n")
```

### Within-Paramormyrops vs Between-Genus Overlap Test

Test whether *Paramormyrops* species show higher dietary overlap with each other than with other genera.

```{r level1-overlap-test}
cat("\n=== WITHIN-PARAMORMYROPS VS. BETWEEN-GENUS OVERLAP TEST ===\n\n")

# Function to categorize overlap comparisons
test_paramormyrops_overlap <- function(overlap_matrix, species_list, primerset_name) {

  cat("\n", primerset_name, ":\n", sep = "")

  if (is.na(overlap_matrix[1]) || is.null(overlap_matrix)) {
    cat("No valid overlap data\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Identify Paramormyrops species
  is_paramormyrops <- grepl("Paramormyrops", species_list)
  param_species <- species_list[is_paramormyrops]

  cat("Paramormyrops species:", length(param_species), "\n")
  cat("Other species:", sum(!is_paramormyrops), "\n")

  # Extract overlap values
  within_param <- c()
  between_genus <- c()

  for (i in 1:(length(species_list)-1)) {
    for (j in (i+1):length(species_list)) {
      sp1 <- species_list[i]
      sp2 <- species_list[j]
      overlap_val <- overlap_matrix[i, j]

      # Both are Paramormyrops
      if (is_paramormyrops[i] && is_paramormyrops[j]) {
        within_param <- c(within_param, overlap_val)
      }
      # One is Paramormyrops, one is not
      else if (is_paramormyrops[i] != is_paramormyrops[j]) {
        between_genus <- c(between_genus, overlap_val)
      }
      # Neither is Paramormyrops - skip
    }
  }

  cat("Within-Paramormyrops comparisons:", length(within_param), "\n")
  cat("Between-genus comparisons:", length(between_genus), "\n")

  if (length(within_param) > 0 && length(between_genus) > 0) {
    cat("\nWithin-Paramormyrops overlap:", round(mean(within_param), 3),
        "±", round(sd(within_param), 3), "\n")
    cat("Between-genus overlap:", round(mean(between_genus), 3),
        "±", round(sd(between_genus), 3), "\n")

    # Wilcoxon test
    wilcox_result <- wilcox.test(within_param, between_genus,
                                  alternative = "greater")

    cat("\nWilcoxon rank-sum test (one-sided):\n")
    cat("W =", wilcox_result$statistic, "\n")
    cat("p-value =", format.pval(wilcox_result$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
    cat("Interpretation:",
        ifelse(wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05,
               "Within-Paramormyrops overlap is significantly higher",
               "No significant difference in overlap"), "\n")

    return(list(within = within_param,
                between = between_genus,
                test = wilcox_result))
  } else {
    cat("Insufficient comparisons for statistical test\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Test for both primer sets (using Schoener index)
test_ps1 <- test_paramormyrops_overlap(overlap_ps1$schoener,
                                       overlap_ps1$species_list,
                                       "PRIMERSET 1")
test_ps2 <- test_paramormyrops_overlap(overlap_ps2$schoener,
                                       overlap_ps2$species_list,
                                       "PRIMERSET 2")
```

### Diet Overlap Heatmaps

```{r level1-overlap-heatmaps, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
library(pheatmap)

# Function to create heatmap for diet overlap
plot_overlap_heatmap <- function(overlap_matrix, title, primerset) {

  if (is.na(overlap_matrix[1]) || is.null(overlap_matrix)) {
    cat("No valid data for", title, "-", primerset, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  if (is.null(rownames(overlap_matrix)) || is.null(colnames(overlap_matrix))) {
    cat("Warning: Missing dimnames for", title, "-", primerset, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  p <- pheatmap(overlap_matrix,
                cluster_rows = TRUE,
                cluster_cols = TRUE,
                display_numbers = TRUE,
                number_format = "%.2f",
                color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "yellow", "orange", "red"))(100),
                main = paste0(title, " (", primerset, ")"),
                fontsize_number = 8,
                fontsize_row = 10,
                fontsize_col = 10,
                angle_col = 45,
                cellwidth = 35,
                cellheight = 35,
                breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = 101))

  return(p)
}

# Create heatmaps for Primerset 1
if (!is.na(overlap_ps1$schoener[1])) {
  plot_overlap_heatmap(overlap_ps1$schoener,
                       "Schoener Diet Overlap",
                       "Primerset 1")
  plot_overlap_heatmap(overlap_ps1$pianka,
                       "Pianka Diet Overlap",
                       "Primerset 1")
}

# Create heatmaps for Primerset 2
if (!is.na(overlap_ps2$schoener[1])) {
  plot_overlap_heatmap(overlap_ps2$schoener,
                       "Schoener Diet Overlap",
                       "Primerset 2")
  plot_overlap_heatmap(overlap_ps2$pianka,
                       "Pianka Diet Overlap",
                       "Primerset 2")
}
```

## 1.5 Phylogenetic Correlation Test

Test whether dietary dissimilarity is correlated with phylogenetic distance (Mantel test).

```{r level1-phylo-correlation}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 1.5: PHYLOGENETIC CORRELATION TEST ===\n\n")

# Function to perform phylogenetic-dietary correlation
test_phylo_diet_correlation <- function(beta_result, phylo_dist_matrix,
                                        phylo_tree_obj, primerset_name) {

  cat("\n", primerset_name, ":\n", sep = "")

  # Get species from beta diversity metadata
  meta <- beta_result$metadata
  species_in_data <- unique(meta$Species)

  cat("Species in dietary data:", length(species_in_data), "\n")

  # Create a temporary phyloseq object to use the matching function
  # We just need the metadata, so create a minimal phyloseq object
  temp_otu <- otu_table(matrix(1, nrow = 1, ncol = nrow(meta)),
                        taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
  colnames(temp_otu) <- rownames(meta)
  temp_meta <- sample_data(meta)
  sample_names(temp_meta) <- rownames(meta)
  temp_ps <- phyloseq(temp_otu, temp_meta)

  # Use the fuzzy matching function to map species to tree tips
  match_results <- match_bale_species_to_phylogeny(temp_ps, phylo_tree_obj)

  cat("Species matched to phylogeny:", match_results$n_matched, "/",
      match_results$n_species, "\n")

  if (match_results$n_matched < 3) {
    cat("Insufficient species overlap with phylogeny (need >= 3)\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Create species-to-tip mapping for matched species
  match_table <- match_results$match_table
  match_table <- match_table[!is.na(match_table$tree_tip), ]

  # Get species that have matches
  shared_species <- match_table$bale_species
  cat("Shared species:", length(shared_species), "\n")
  cat("  ", paste(shared_species, collapse = ", "), "\n")

  # Create species-level dietary distance matrix
  # Average dietary distance between all samples of each species pair
  diet_dist_species <- matrix(0, nrow = length(shared_species),
                              ncol = length(shared_species))
  rownames(diet_dist_species) <- shared_species
  colnames(diet_dist_species) <- shared_species

  for (i in 1:length(shared_species)) {
    for (j in 1:length(shared_species)) {
      if (i != j) {
        # Get samples for species i and j
        samples_i <- rownames(meta)[meta$Species == shared_species[i]]
        samples_j <- rownames(meta)[meta$Species == shared_species[j]]

        # Extract pairwise distances from distance matrix
        dist_mat <- as.matrix(beta_result$distance)
        pairwise_dists <- dist_mat[samples_i, samples_j]

        # Average distance between species
        diet_dist_species[i, j] <- mean(pairwise_dists, na.rm = TRUE)
      }
    }
  }

  # Convert to dist object
  diet_dist_species <- as.dist(diet_dist_species)

  # Create phylogenetic distance matrix using tree tips
  tree_tips <- match_table$tree_tip
  names(tree_tips) <- match_table$bale_species

  # Extract phylogenetic distances for matched tips
  phylo_dist_tips <- phylo_dist_matrix[tree_tips, tree_tips]

  # Rename to species names for consistency
  rownames(phylo_dist_tips) <- names(tree_tips)
  colnames(phylo_dist_tips) <- names(tree_tips)

  # Ensure same order as diet_dist_species
  phylo_dist_tips <- phylo_dist_tips[shared_species, shared_species]
  phylo_dist_shared <- as.dist(phylo_dist_tips)

  # Mantel test
  mantel_result <- vegan::mantel(diet_dist_species, phylo_dist_shared,
                                 method = "pearson", permutations = 999)

  cat("\nMantel Test Results:\n")
  cat("Mantel statistic (r):", round(mantel_result$statistic, 3), "\n")
  cat("P-value:", format.pval(mantel_result$signif, digits = 3), "\n")
  cat("Interpretation:",
      ifelse(mantel_result$signif < 0.05,
             "Significant phylogenetic signal in diet",
             "No significant phylogenetic signal in diet"), "\n")

  return(list(
    mantel = mantel_result,
    diet_dist = diet_dist_species,
    phylo_dist = phylo_dist_shared,
    shared_species = shared_species,
    match_table = match_table
  ))
}

# Test for both primer sets (if phylogeny is available)
if (!is.null(phylo_dist) && !is.null(phylo_tree)) {
  phylo_cor_ps1 <- test_phylo_diet_correlation(beta1_bale, phylo_dist,
                                                phylo_tree, "PRIMERSET 1")
  phylo_cor_ps2 <- test_phylo_diet_correlation(beta2_bale, phylo_dist,
                                                phylo_tree, "PRIMERSET 2")
} else {
  cat("Phylogenetic data not available. Skipping analysis.\n")
  phylo_cor_ps1 <- NULL
  phylo_cor_ps2 <- NULL
}
```

## 1.6 SIMPER Analysis - Species

Similarity Percentage (SIMPER) analysis identifies which prey taxa contribute most to dietary differences among species.

```{r level1-simper, results='asis',out.width="80%"}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 1.6: SIMPER ANALYSIS - SPECIES ===\n\n")

# Function to run and summarize SIMPER
run_simper_analysis <- function(ps, primerset_name, top_n = 10,
                                detailed_output = FALSE) {

  cat("\n", primerset_name, ":\n", sep = "")

  # Get OTU table (samples as rows, taxa as columns)
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps))
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
    otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
  }

  # Get metadata
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))

  # Run SIMPER
  simper_result <- vegan::simper(otu_mat, meta$Species, permutations = 999)

  cat("\nSIMPER analysis complete\n")
  cat("Number of species comparisons:", length(simper_result), "\n")

  # Extract top contributors for each comparison
  simper_summary <- summary(simper_result)

  # Create consolidated heatmap or detailed tables based on parameter
  if (!detailed_output) {
    cat("\nCreating consolidated heatmap of top prey families...\n")
    heatmap_plot <- plot_simper_heatmap(simper_result, ps,
                                        top_n = 15,
                                        primerset_name = primerset_name)
    print(heatmap_plot)
    cat("\nDetailed tables suppressed. Set detailed_output = TRUE to view.\n\n")
  } else {
    cat("\nTop", top_n, "prey taxa contributing to species differences:\n\n")

    # Print summary for each pairwise comparison
    for (comparison_name in names(simper_summary)) {
    # Sanitize comparison name for LaTeX
    comparison_name_clean <- gsub("_", " vs ", comparison_name)
    cat("###", comparison_name_clean, "\n\n")

    comparison_data <- simper_summary[[comparison_name]]

    # Check structure and convert if needed
    if (!is.data.frame(comparison_data)) {
      comparison_data <- as.data.frame(comparison_data)
    }

    # Skip if no data
    if (nrow(comparison_data) == 0) {
      cat("Warning: No data for this comparison. Skipping.\n\n")
      next
    }

    # Add contr column (use 'average' which is the contribution to dissimilarity)
    # The cumsum column already exists from vegan's summary()
    comparison_data$contr <- comparison_data$average

    # Filter to cumulative contribution < 70%
    # Note: cumsum is already calculated by vegan, but it's as a proportion
    # We need to recalculate based on percentage contribution
    total_dissim <- sum(comparison_data$average, na.rm = TRUE)
    comparison_data$pct_contrib <- (comparison_data$average / total_dissim) * 100
    comparison_data$cumsum_pct <- cumsum(comparison_data$pct_contrib)

    top_taxa <- comparison_data[comparison_data$cumsum_pct <= 70, ]

    # If nothing meets 70%, take top 10
    if (nrow(top_taxa) == 0) {
      top_taxa <- head(comparison_data, top_n)
    }

    # Get taxonomy for these ASVs
    tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
    top_taxa$ASV <- rownames(top_taxa)

    # Add taxonomic names
    top_taxa$Family <- sapply(top_taxa$ASV, function(asv) {
      if (asv %in% rownames(tax_df)) {
        fam <- tax_df[asv, "Family"]
        if (is.na(fam) || fam == "") {
          return(tax_df[asv, "Order"])
        }
        return(clean_family_name(fam))
      }
      return("Unknown")
    })

    # Aggregate ASVs by Family for plotting
    family_aggregated <- top_taxa %>%
      group_by(Family) %>%
      summarise(
        pct_contrib = sum(pct_contrib),
        n_asvs = n(),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      arrange(desc(pct_contrib))

    # Select columns for display (ASV-level detail)
    display_table <- top_taxa[, c("Family", "average", "sd", "ratio",
                                  "ava", "avb", "pct_contrib",
                                  "cumsum_pct")]

    # Rename for clarity
    colnames(display_table)[colnames(display_table) == "pct_contrib"] <- "% Contrib"
    colnames(display_table)[colnames(display_table) == "cumsum_pct"] <- "Cum %"

    # Round numeric columns
    display_table[, c("average", "sd", "ratio", "ava", "avb",
                      "% Contrib", "Cum %")] <-
      round(display_table[, c("average", "sd", "ratio", "ava", "avb",
                              "% Contrib", "Cum %")], 3)

    cat(paste0("\n#### SIMPER: ", comparison_name_clean, "\n\n"))
    # Limit to top 10 rows for space
    display_table_top <- head(display_table, 10)
    kable(display_table_top,
                caption = paste("SIMPER:", comparison_name_clean, "(Top 10)"),
                row.names = FALSE,
                booktabs = TRUE) %>%
            kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position"),
                          bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                          full_width = FALSE, font_size = 8)

    # Create family-aggregated plot
    plot_data <- head(family_aggregated, 15)
    plot_data$Family <- factor(plot_data$Family,
                               levels = rev(plot_data$Family))

    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = pct_contrib, y = Family)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#0072B2", alpha = 0.7) +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(title = paste0("Top Prey Families: ", comparison_name_clean,
                          " (", primerset_name, ")"),
           x = "% Contribution to Dissimilarity",
           y = "Prey Family") +
      theme()

    print(p)

    cat("\n\n")
    }
  }

  return(simper_result)
}

# Run SIMPER for both primer sets (heatmap mode by default)
simper_ps1 <- run_simper_analysis(ps1_bale_filt, "PRIMERSET 1",
                                  detailed_output = FALSE)
simper_ps2 <- run_simper_analysis(ps2_bale_filt, "PRIMERSET 2",
                                  detailed_output = FALSE)
```

---

# LEVEL 2: BP vs. TP Mormyrids Globally

**Biological context:** All mormyrid species across all sites (97-99 individuals). This level examines the broadest dietary differences associated with EOD waveform type (biphasic vs. triphasic) across the entire mormyrid community.

## 2.1 Prey Composition by Waveform

Family-level prey composition comparing BP and TP waveform mormyrids globally.

```{r level2-prey-composition}
cat("=== LEVEL 2.1: PREY COMPOSITION BY WAVEFORM (FAMILY LEVEL) ===\n\n")

# Aggregate to family level
ps1_all_family <- aggregate_to_family(ps1_all_mormyrids)
ps2_all_family <- aggregate_to_family(ps2_all_mormyrids)

cat("Family-level aggregation:\n")
cat("  PS1:", ntaxa(ps1_all_family), "families\n")
cat("  PS2:", ntaxa(ps2_all_family), "families\n")

# Calculate summary statistics by waveform type
ps1_all_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps1_all_family, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)
ps2_all_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps2_all_family, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)

# Function to calculate family summary by waveform
calc_family_summary_waveform <- function(ps_rel) {
  ps_melt <- psmelt(ps_rel)
  ps_melt$Family_clean <- sapply(ps_melt$Family, clean_family_name)

  summary_table <- ps_melt %>%
    group_by(Family_clean, Waveform_Type) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_pct = mean(Abundance),
      se_pct = sd(Abundance) / sqrt(n()),
      n_samples = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_pct)) %>%
    mutate(mean_se = paste0(round(mean_pct, 2), " ± ", round(se_pct, 2)))

  return(summary_table)
}

summary_wave_ps1 <- calc_family_summary_waveform(ps1_all_rel)
summary_wave_ps2 <- calc_family_summary_waveform(ps2_all_rel)

cat("\nTop 15 prey families by waveform (Primer Set 1):\n")
print(head(summary_wave_ps1, 30))

cat("\nTop 15 prey families by waveform (Primer Set 2):\n")
print(head(summary_wave_ps2, 30))

# Identify top families across both primer sets for unified coloring
```
