```{r level2-prey-composition}
combined_summary_wave <- bind_rows(
  summary_wave_ps1 %>% mutate(Dataset = "PS1"),
  summary_wave_ps2 %>% mutate(Dataset = "PS2")
) %>%
  group_by(Family_clean) %>%
  summarise(Mean_Prop = mean(mean_pct), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_Prop)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

top_families_wave_unified <- combined_summary_wave$Family_clean

cat("\nTop families (unified across both primer sets):\n")
print(top_families_wave_unified)

# Ensure all top families have colors assigned in global palette
missing_families_wave <- setdiff(top_families_wave_unified, names(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS))
if (length(missing_families_wave) > 0) {
  cat("Adding colors for", length(missing_families_wave), "families not in global palette:\n")
  print(missing_families_wave)

  # Find colors not yet used
  used_colors <- GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS[names(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS) != "Other"]
  available_colors <- setdiff(distinct_colors, used_colors)

  # Add colors for missing families
  new_colors <- setNames(
    available_colors[1:length(missing_families_wave)],
    missing_families_wave
  )
  GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS <- c(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS, new_colors)
}

# Create stacked bar plots with unified family list
p1_wave <- plot_composition_unified(ps1_all_family,
                                    group_var = "Waveform_Type",
                                    families_to_show = top_families_wave_unified,
                                    title = "Prey Composition by Waveform (PS1)")

p2_wave <- plot_composition_unified(ps2_all_family,
                                    group_var = "Waveform_Type",
                                    families_to_show = top_families_wave_unified,
                                    title = "Prey Composition by Waveform (PS2)")

# Display side by side with unified legend using cowplot
p1_wave_no_legend <- p1_wave + theme(legend.position = "none")
p2_wave_no_legend <- p2_wave + theme(legend.position = "none")

# Create dummy plot for unified legend
dummy_data_wave <- data.frame(
  x = rep("A", length(top_families_wave_unified) + 1),
  y = rep(1, length(top_families_wave_unified) + 1),
  Family = factor(c(top_families_wave_unified, "Other"),
                  levels = c(top_families_wave_unified, "Other"))
)

p_dummy_wave <- ggplot(dummy_data_wave, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = Family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS,
                    breaks = c(top_families_wave_unified, "Other"),
                    name = "Prey Family",
                    drop = FALSE) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 5, byrow = TRUE, title = "Prey Family"))

legend_wave <- get_legend(p_dummy_wave)

# Combine plots and legend
plot_grid(
  plot_grid(p1_wave_no_legend, p2_wave_no_legend, ncol = 2),
  legend_wave,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.15)
)
```

Summary table of top prey families by waveform:

```{r level2-prey-table}
# Create combined summary table
top_families_wave <- summary_wave_ps1 %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = Family_clean,
              names_from = Waveform_Type,
              values_from = c(mean_pct, se_pct),
              values_fill = list(mean_pct = 0, se_pct = 0)) %>%
  arrange(desc(pmax(mean_pct_BP, mean_pct_TP, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

kable(top_families_wave,
      caption = "Top 15 prey families by waveform type (mean $\\pm$ SE relative abundance \\%)",
      digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Family", "Mean \\% BP", "Mean \\% TP", "SE BP", "SE TP"),
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## 2.2 Alpha Diversity: BP vs. TP

Shannon diversity analysis comparing BP and TP waveform mormyrids globally.

```{r level2-alpha-diversity, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
cat("=== LEVEL 2.2: ALPHA DIVERSITY - BP vs TP ===\n\n")

# Analyze alpha diversity for both primer sets
alpha1_wave <- analyze_alpha_diversity(ps1_all_mormyrids, "Waveform_Type")
alpha2_wave <- analyze_alpha_diversity(ps2_all_mormyrids, "Waveform_Type")

# Calculate summary statistics
cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 1 - BP vs TP Shannon Diversity ===\n")
bp_mean_ps1 <- mean(alpha1_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha1_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "BP"], na.rm = TRUE)
bp_sd_ps1 <- sd(alpha1_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha1_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "BP"], na.rm = TRUE)
tp_mean_ps1 <- mean(alpha1_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha1_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "TP"], na.rm = TRUE)
tp_sd_ps1 <- sd(alpha1_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha1_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "TP"], na.rm = TRUE)

cat("BP Shannon (mean ± SD):", round(bp_mean_ps1, 3), "±", round(bp_sd_ps1, 3), "\n")
cat("TP Shannon (mean ± SD):", round(tp_mean_ps1, 3), "±", round(tp_sd_ps1, 3), "\n")

cat("\nTest type:", alpha1_wave$test_type, "\n")
cat("Shapiro-Wilk p-value:", round(alpha1_wave$shapiro_p, 4), "\n")
if (alpha1_wave$test_type == "Kruskal-Wallis") {
  print(alpha1_wave$test)
} else {
  print(summary(alpha1_wave$test))
}

# Cohen's d effect size
cat("\nEffect size (Cohen's d):\n")
bp_vals_ps1 <- alpha1_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha1_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "BP"]
tp_vals_ps1 <- alpha1_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha1_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "TP"]
cohen_ps1 <- effsize::cohen.d(bp_vals_ps1, tp_vals_ps1, na.rm = TRUE)
print(cohen_ps1)

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 2 - BP vs TP Shannon Diversity ===\n")
bp_mean_ps2 <- mean(alpha2_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha2_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "BP"], na.rm = TRUE)
bp_sd_ps2 <- sd(alpha2_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha2_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "BP"], na.rm = TRUE)
tp_mean_ps2 <- mean(alpha2_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha2_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "TP"], na.rm = TRUE)
tp_sd_ps2 <- sd(alpha2_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha2_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "TP"], na.rm = TRUE)

cat("BP Shannon (mean ± SD):", round(bp_mean_ps2, 3), "±", round(bp_sd_ps2, 3), "\n")
cat("TP Shannon (mean ± SD):", round(tp_mean_ps2, 3), "±", round(tp_sd_ps2, 3), "\n")

cat("\nTest type:", alpha2_wave$test_type, "\n")
cat("Shapiro-Wilk p-value:", round(alpha2_wave$shapiro_p, 4), "\n")
if (alpha2_wave$test_type == "Kruskal-Wallis") {
  print(alpha2_wave$test)
} else {
  print(summary(alpha2_wave$test))
}

# Cohen's d effect size
cat("\nEffect size (Cohen's d):\n")
bp_vals_ps2 <- alpha2_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha2_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "BP"]
tp_vals_ps2 <- alpha2_wave$diversity$Shannon[alpha2_wave$diversity$Waveform_Type == "TP"]
cohen_ps2 <- effsize::cohen.d(bp_vals_ps2, tp_vals_ps2, na.rm = TRUE)
print(cohen_ps2)

# Comparative plot
p_wave_alpha_comp <- plot_comparative_shannon(alpha1_wave$diversity,
                                             alpha2_wave$diversity,
                                             "Waveform_Type",
                                             "Global BP vs TP Diet Diversity Comparison")
print(p_wave_alpha_comp)
```

### Cross-Primer Correlation for Alpha Diversity

```{r level2-alpha-correlation, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Correlate Shannon values for shared samples
cor_wave_alpha <- correlate_shared_samples(alpha1_wave$diversity,
                                          alpha2_wave$diversity)

cat("\n=== GLOBAL WAVEFORM ALPHA DIVERSITY CORRELATION ===\n")
cat("Shared samples:", cor_wave_alpha$n_shared, "\n")

if (!is.null(cor_wave_alpha$cor_test)) {
  cat("Pearson r:", round(cor_wave_alpha$cor_test$estimate, 3), "\n")
  cat("95% CI: [", round(cor_wave_alpha$cor_test$conf.int[1], 3), ",",
      round(cor_wave_alpha$cor_test$conf.int[2], 3), "]\n")
  cat("P-value:", format.pval(cor_wave_alpha$cor_test$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")

  # Scatterplot - get shared samples
  shared_ids <- intersect(alpha1_wave$diversity$Sample_Name,
                          alpha2_wave$diversity$Sample_Name)

  # Filter to shared samples and match order
  div1_shared <- alpha1_wave$diversity %>%
    filter(Sample_Name %in% shared_ids) %>%
    arrange(Sample_Name)
  div2_shared <- alpha2_wave$diversity %>%
    filter(Sample_Name %in% shared_ids) %>%
    arrange(Sample_Name)

  cor_df <- data.frame(
    PS1_Shannon = div1_shared$Shannon,
    PS2_Shannon = div2_shared$Shannon
  )

  p_alpha_cor <- ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = PS1_Shannon, y = PS2_Shannon)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6, color = ps1_color) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "gray50") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Global Waveform: Shannon Diversity Correlation",
         subtitle = paste0("r = ", round(cor_wave_alpha$cor_test$estimate, 3),
                          ", p ", format.pval(cor_wave_alpha$cor_test$p.value, digits = 2)),
         x = "Primerset 1 Shannon", y = "Primerset 2 Shannon") +
    coord_fixed()

  print(p_alpha_cor)
} else {
  cat(cor_wave_alpha$message, "\n")
}
```

## 2.3 Beta Diversity: BP vs. TP

Bray-Curtis dissimilarity analysis with PERMANOVA testing for dietary differences between BP and TP waveform types globally.

```{r level2-beta-diversity, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 2.3: BETA DIVERSITY - BP vs TP ===\n\n")

# Run beta diversity analyses
beta1_wave <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps1_all_mormyrids, "~ Waveform_Type")
beta2_wave <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps2_all_mormyrids, "~ Waveform_Type")

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 1 - Global Waveform PERMANOVA ===\n")
print(beta1_wave$permanova)

# Extract R² for reporting
r2_ps1 <- beta1_wave$permanova$R2[1]
p_ps1 <- beta1_wave$permanova$`Pr(>F)`[1]
cat("\nR² =", round(r2_ps1, 4), ", p =", format.pval(p_ps1, digits = 3), "\n")

cat("\n=== PRIMERSET 2 - Global Waveform PERMANOVA ===\n")
print(beta2_wave$permanova)

# Extract R² for reporting
r2_ps2 <- beta2_wave$permanova$R2[1]
p_ps2 <- beta2_wave$permanova$`Pr(>F)`[1]
cat("\nR² =", round(r2_ps2, 4), ", p =", format.pval(p_ps2, digits = 3), "\n")

# Faceted PCoA plot
p_wave_pcoa <- plot_faceted_pcoa(beta1_wave, beta2_wave,
                                color_var = "Waveform_Type",
                                title = "Global Diet Composition: BP vs TP Waveform")
print(p_wave_pcoa)
```

### Beta Diversity Cross-Validation (Mantel Test)

```{r level2-beta-mantel}
# Find shared Sample_Names between datasets
samples1_wave <- beta1_wave$metadata$Sample_Name
samples2_wave <- beta2_wave$metadata$Sample_Name
shared_wave <- intersect(samples1_wave, samples2_wave)

cat("\n=== GLOBAL WAVEFORM BETA DIVERSITY MANTEL TEST ===\n")
cat("Shared Sample_Names:", length(shared_wave), "\n")

if (length(shared_wave) >= 3) {
  # Convert distance matrices to matrices with Sample_Name labels
  dist1_mat <- as.matrix(beta1_wave$distance)
  dist2_mat <- as.matrix(beta2_wave$distance)

  # Relabel with Sample_Names
  rownames(dist1_mat) <- beta1_wave$metadata$Sample_Name
  colnames(dist1_mat) <- beta1_wave$metadata$Sample_Name
  rownames(dist2_mat) <- beta2_wave$metadata$Sample_Name
  colnames(dist2_mat) <- beta2_wave$metadata$Sample_Name

  # Subset to shared Sample_Names (automatically aligned)
  dist1_shared <- as.dist(dist1_mat[shared_wave, shared_wave])
  dist2_shared <- as.dist(dist2_mat[shared_wave, shared_wave])

  # Mantel test
  mantel_wave <- run_mantel_test(dist1_shared, dist2_shared)

  cat("\nMantel statistic (r):", round(mantel_wave$statistic, 3), "\n")
  cat("P-value:", format.pval(mantel_wave$signif, digits = 3), "\n")
  cat("Interpretation:",
      ifelse(mantel_wave$statistic > 0.5, "Strong concordance",
             ifelse(mantel_wave$statistic > 0.3, "Moderate concordance",
                    "Weak concordance")), "\n")
} else {
  cat("Insufficient shared samples for Mantel test.\n")
  mantel_wave <- NULL
}
```

## 2.4 SIMPER Analysis - Waveform

Similarity Percentage (SIMPER) analysis identifies which prey families contribute most to dietary differences between BP and TP waveform types.

```{r level2-simper, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 2.4: SIMPER ANALYSIS - WAVEFORM ===\n\n")

# Function to run SIMPER and create visualization
run_simper_waveform <- function(ps, primerset_name) {

  cat("\n", primerset_name, ":\n", sep = "")

  # Get OTU table (samples as rows, taxa as columns)
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps))
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
    otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
  }

  # Get metadata
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))

  # Run SIMPER
  simper_result <- vegan::simper(otu_mat, meta$Waveform_Type, permutations = 999)

  cat("\nSIMPER analysis complete\n")

  # Extract results for BP vs TP comparison
  # There should be only one comparison (BP_TP or TP_BP)
  comparison_name <- names(simper_result)[1]
  comparison_name_clean <- gsub("_", " vs ", comparison_name)
  comp <- simper_result[[comparison_name]]

  cat("\nComparison:", comparison_name_clean, "\n")

  # Extract overall dissimilarity
  overall_diss <- comp$overall
  cat("Overall average dissimilarity:", round(overall_diss, 3), "\n")

  # Build data frame from simper result
  comparison_data <- data.frame(
    species = comp$species,
    average = comp$average,
    sd = comp$sd,
    ratio = comp$ratio,
    ava = comp$ava,
    avb = comp$avb,
    p = comp$p,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Calculate contribution percentage (average contribution / overall dissimilarity)
  comparison_data$contr <- comparison_data$average / overall_diss

  # Sort by contribution (descending)
  comparison_data <- comparison_data[order(-comparison_data$contr), ]
  rownames(comparison_data) <- comparison_data$species

  # Calculate cumulative sum
  comparison_data$cumsum <- cumsum(comparison_data$contr)

  # Get top contributors (cumulative contribution up to 70%)
  top_taxa <- comparison_data[comparison_data$cumsum <= 0.70, ]

  # If nothing meets 70%, take top 15
  if (nrow(top_taxa) == 0) {
    top_taxa <- head(comparison_data, 15)
  }

  # Get taxonomy for these ASVs
  tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
  top_taxa$ASV <- rownames(top_taxa)

  # Add taxonomic names (Family level)
  top_taxa$Family <- sapply(top_taxa$ASV, function(asv) {
    if (asv %in% rownames(tax_df)) {
      fam <- tax_df[asv, "Family"]
      if (is.na(fam) || fam == "") {
        return(tax_df[asv, "Order"])
      }
      return(clean_family_name(fam))
    }
    return("Unknown")
  })

  # Aggregate ASVs by Family for plotting
  family_aggregated <- top_taxa %>%
    group_by(Family) %>%
    summarise(
      contr = sum(contr),
      average = sum(average),
      n_asvs = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(contr))

  # Print table (ASV-level detail)
  display_table <- top_taxa[, c("Family", "average", "sd", "ratio",
                                "ava", "avb", "contr", "cumsum")]

  # Round numeric columns
  display_table[, c("average", "sd", "ratio", "ava", "avb",
                    "contr", "cumsum")] <-
    round(display_table[, c("average", "sd", "ratio", "ava", "avb",
                            "contr", "cumsum")], 3)

  cat(paste0("\n#### SIMPER: ", comparison_name_clean, " (", primerset_name, ")\n\n"))
  # Limit to top 10 rows for space
  display_table_top <- head(display_table, 10)
  kable(display_table_top,
              caption = paste0("SIMPER: ", comparison_name_clean, " (", primerset_name, ") - Top 10"),
              row.names = FALSE,
              booktabs = TRUE) %>%
          kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position"),
                        bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                        full_width = FALSE, font_size = 8)

  # Create bar plot of contribution percentage - FAMILY LEVEL
  plot_data <- head(family_aggregated, 15)

  # Create factor for ordering (highest contribution at top)
  plot_data$Family <- factor(plot_data$Family,
                             levels = rev(plot_data$Family))

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = contr * 100, y = Family)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#0072B2", alpha = 0.7) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste0("Top Prey Families Contributing to BP vs TP Dissimilarity (", primerset_name, ")"),
         x = "Contribution to Dissimilarity (%)",
         y = "Prey Family")

  return(list(simper = simper_result, plot = p))
}

# Run SIMPER for both primer sets
simper_wave_ps1 <- run_simper_waveform(ps1_all_mormyrids, "PRIMERSET 1")
simper_wave_ps2 <- run_simper_waveform(ps2_all_mormyrids, "PRIMERSET 2")

# Display plots
print(simper_wave_ps1$plot)
print(simper_wave_ps2$plot)
```

---

# LEVEL 3: Paramormyrops kingsleyae - General Population Factors

**Biological context:** ~47 *P. kingsleyae* individuals from 4 rivers (2 regions × 2 waveform types). This level examines how sex, body size, parasitism, and geography affect diet before testing the core BP vs TP question in Level 4.

## 3.1 Prey Composition by Population

Family-level prey composition across the four river populations, organized by region and waveform type to facilitate comparisons.

```{r level3-prey-composition, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
cat("=== LEVEL 3.1: PREY COMPOSITION BY POPULATION ===\n\n")

# Aggregate to family level
ps1_pk_family <- aggregate_to_family(ps1_pk)
ps2_pk_family <- aggregate_to_family(ps2_pk)

cat("Family-level aggregation (P. kingsleyae):\n")
cat("  PS1:", ntaxa(ps1_pk_family), "families\n")
cat("  PS2:", ntaxa(ps2_pk_family), "families\n")

# Add Region variable for faceting
add_region_variable <- function(ps) {
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))

  # Define regions based on locality
  meta$Region <- ifelse(meta$Locality_Name %in% c("Bambomo", "Apassa"),
                        "South", "North")

  # Update sample data
  sample_data(ps) <- meta
  return(ps)
}

ps1_pk_family <- add_region_variable(ps1_pk_family)
ps2_pk_family <- add_region_variable(ps2_pk_family)

# Create 4-panel stacked bar chart function
create_4panel_composition <- function(ps_family, primerset_name, top_n = 10,
                                      families_to_show = NULL,
                                      use_global_colors = TRUE) {

  # Convert to relative abundance
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_family, function(x) x / sum(x))

  # Melt to long format
  ps_melt <- psmelt(ps_rel)

  # Clean family names
  ps_melt$Family_clean <- sapply(ps_melt$Family, clean_family_name)

  # Identify top N families by mean abundance
  if (is.null(families_to_show)) {
    top_families <- ps_melt %>%
      group_by(Family_clean) %>%
      summarise(mean_abund = mean(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(desc(mean_abund)) %>%
      slice_head(n = top_n) %>%
      pull(Family_clean)
  } else {
    top_families <- families_to_show
  }

  # Aggregate by Locality_Name and Family_clean (sum across samples)
  ps_agg <- ps_melt %>%
    group_by(Locality_Name, Region, Waveform_Type, Family_clean) %>%
    summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

  # Normalize within each Locality_Name so each bar sums to 1 (100%)
  ps_agg <- ps_agg %>%
    group_by(Locality_Name) %>%
    mutate(RelAbund = Abundance / sum(Abundance)) %>%
    ungroup()

  # Categorize families into top families vs "Other"
  ps_final <- ps_agg %>%
    mutate(Family_display = ifelse(Family_clean %in% top_families,
                                   Family_clean, "Other")) %>%
    group_by(Locality_Name, Region, Waveform_Type, Family_display) %>%
    summarise(RelAbund = sum(RelAbund), .groups = "drop")

  # Reorder for plotting
  ps_final$Family_display <- factor(ps_final$Family_display,
                                     levels = c(top_families, "Other"))

  # Create plot with facet_grid(Region ~ Waveform_Type)
  p <- ggplot(ps_final, aes(x = Locality_Name, y = RelAbund,
                            fill = Family_display)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
    facet_grid(Region ~ Waveform_Type, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("P. kingsleyae Prey Composition by Population -", primerset_name),
      subtitle = "Organized by Region (North/South) and Waveform (BP/TP)",
      x = "River",
      y = "Relative Abundance"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)

  # Add color scale
  if (use_global_colors) {
    p <- p + scale_fill_manual(values = GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS,
                               name = "Prey Family",
                               breaks = c(top_families, "Other"),
                               drop = FALSE)
  } else {
    p <- p + scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", name = "Prey Family")
  }

  return(p)
}

# Identify top families across both primer sets for unified coloring
# Calculate summaries for both datasets
ps1_pk_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps1_pk_family, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)
ps2_pk_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps2_pk_family, function(x) x / sum(x) * 100)

# Get family summaries
get_family_summary_pk <- function(ps_rel) {
  ps_melt <- psmelt(ps_rel)
  ps_melt$Family_clean <- sapply(ps_melt$Family, clean_family_name)

  summary_table <- ps_melt %>%
    group_by(Family_clean) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_pct = mean(Abundance),
      sd_pct = sd(Abundance),
      n_samples = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_pct)) %>%
    mutate(mean_sd = paste0(round(mean_pct, 2), " ± ", round(sd_pct, 2)))

  return(summary_table)
}

summary_pk_ps1 <- get_family_summary_pk(ps1_pk_rel)
summary_pk_ps2 <- get_family_summary_pk(ps2_pk_rel)

# Combine and get top families
combined_summary_pk <- bind_rows(
  summary_pk_ps1 %>% mutate(Dataset = "PS1"),
  summary_pk_ps2 %>% mutate(Dataset = "PS2")
) %>%
  group_by(Family_clean) %>%
  summarise(Mean_Prop = mean(mean_pct), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_Prop)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

top_families_pk_unified <- combined_summary_pk$Family_clean

cat("\nTop families for P. kingsleyae (unified across both primer sets):\n")
print(top_families_pk_unified)

# Ensure all top families have colors assigned in global palette
missing_families_pk <- setdiff(top_families_pk_unified, names(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS))
if (length(missing_families_pk) > 0) {
  cat("Adding colors for", length(missing_families_pk), "families not in global palette:\n")
  print(missing_families_pk)

  # Find colors not yet used
  used_colors <- GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS[names(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS) != "Other"]
  available_colors <- setdiff(distinct_colors, used_colors)

  # Add colors for missing families
  new_colors <- setNames(
    available_colors[1:length(missing_families_pk)],
    missing_families_pk
  )
  GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS <- c(GLOBAL_FAMILY_COLORS, new_colors)
}

# Create plots for both primer sets with unified families
p_comp_ps1 <- create_4panel_composition(ps1_pk_family, "Primerset 1",
                                        families_to_show = top_families_pk_unified,
                                        use_global_colors = TRUE)
p_comp_ps2 <- create_4panel_composition(ps2_pk_family, "Primerset 2",
                                        families_to_show = top_families_pk_unified,
                                        use_global_colors = TRUE)

print(p_comp_ps1)
cat("\n\n")
print(p_comp_ps2)

# Summary table by river
cat("\n=== SUMMARY BY RIVER ===\n")
pk_meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps1_pk))
pk_summary <- pk_meta %>%
  group_by(Locality_Name, Region, Waveform_Type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(Region, Waveform_Type)

kable(pk_summary,
      caption = "P. kingsleyae sampling by river",
      col.names = c("River", "Region", "Waveform", "N"),
      booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE)
```

## 3.2 Sex Effects on Shannon Diversity

Test for sex differences in diet diversity using ANCOVA with body size as a covariate to account for sexual size dimorphism.

```{r level3-sex-effects, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 3.2: SEX EFFECTS ON SHANNON DIVERSITY ===\n\n")

# Prepare data with sex and size
prepare_sex_size_data <- function(ps, size_col = "SL") {
  meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))
  div <- estimate_richness(ps, measures = c("Shannon"))

  div$Sex <- meta$Sex
  div$body_size <- as.numeric(meta[[size_col]])
  div$Sample_Name <- meta$Sample_Name
  div$Locality_Name <- meta$Locality_Name
  div$Waveform_Type <- meta$Waveform_Type

  # Remove missing data
  div <- div[!is.na(div$Sex) & !is.na(div$body_size), ]

  return(div)
}

div_ps1_pk <- prepare_sex_size_data(ps1_pk, "SL")
div_ps2_pk <- prepare_sex_size_data(ps2_pk, "SL")

cat("Sample sizes (P. kingsleyae):\n")
cat("  PS1:", nrow(div_ps1_pk), "samples\n")
if (nrow(div_ps1_pk) > 0) {
  cat("  PS1 sex distribution:\n")
  print(table(div_ps1_pk$Sex))
}

cat("\n  PS2:", nrow(div_ps2_pk), "samples\n")
if (nrow(div_ps2_pk) > 0) {
  cat("  PS2 sex distribution:\n")
  print(table(div_ps2_pk$Sex))
}

# Filter to adults only for sex comparison (exclude juveniles)
div_ps1_pk_adults <- div_ps1_pk[div_ps1_pk$Sex %in% c("Male", "Female"), ]
div_ps2_pk_adults <- div_ps2_pk[div_ps2_pk$Sex %in% c("Male", "Female"), ]

cat("\nAdult samples (excluding juveniles):\n")
cat("  PS1:", nrow(div_ps1_pk_adults), "adults\n")
cat("  PS2:", nrow(div_ps2_pk_adults), "adults\n")

# Check correlation between sex and size in adults
if (nrow(div_ps1_pk_adults) > 5 && length(unique(div_ps1_pk_adults$Sex)) == 2) {
  cat("\n=== SEX-SIZE CORRELATION (P. kingsleyae ADULTS) ===\n")

  # PS1
  cat("\nPRIMERSET 1:\n")
  cat("Body size by sex (mean ± SD):\n")
  size_by_sex_ps1 <- aggregate(body_size ~ Sex, data = div_ps1_pk_adults,
                                FUN = function(x) {
                                  paste0(round(mean(x), 1), " ± ",
                                         round(sd(x), 1))
                                })
  print(size_by_sex_ps1)

  size_test_ps1 <- t.test(body_size ~ Sex, data = div_ps1_pk_adults)
  cat("t-test p-value:", format.pval(size_test_ps1$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")

  # PS2
  if (nrow(div_ps2_pk_adults) > 5 && length(unique(div_ps2_pk_adults$Sex)) == 2) {
    cat("\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
    cat("Body size by sex (mean ± SD):\n")
    size_by_sex_ps2 <- aggregate(body_size ~ Sex, data = div_ps2_pk_adults,
                                  FUN = function(x) {
                                    paste0(round(mean(x), 1), " ± ",
                                           round(sd(x), 1))
                                  })
    print(size_by_sex_ps2)

    size_test_ps2 <- t.test(body_size ~ Sex, data = div_ps2_pk_adults)
    cat("t-test p-value:", format.pval(size_test_ps2$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
  }

  # ANCOVA models (sex + body size)
  cat("\n=== ANCOVA MODELS (ADULTS ONLY) ===\n")
  cat("Model: Shannon ~ Sex + body_size + Sex:body_size\n\n")

  # PS1 ANCOVA
  cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
  ancova_sex_ps1 <- lm(Shannon ~ Sex + body_size + Sex:body_size,
                       data = div_ps1_pk_adults)
  print(summary(ancova_sex_ps1))
  cat("\nANOVA table:\n")
  print(anova(ancova_sex_ps1))

  # PS2 ANCOVA
  if (nrow(div_ps2_pk_adults) > 5 && length(unique(div_ps2_pk_adults$Sex)) == 2) {
    cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
    ancova_sex_ps2 <- lm(Shannon ~ Sex + body_size + Sex:body_size,
                         data = div_ps2_pk_adults)
    print(summary(ancova_sex_ps2))
    cat("\nANOVA table:\n")
    print(anova(ancova_sex_ps2))
  }

  # Visualization
  div_ps1_pk_adults$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 1"
  div_ps2_pk_adults$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 2"
  div_combined_adults <- bind_rows(div_ps1_pk_adults, div_ps2_pk_adults)

  # Also prepare all-individuals dataset
  div_ps1_pk$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 1"
  div_ps2_pk$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 2"
  div_combined_all <- bind_rows(div_ps1_pk, div_ps2_pk)

  # Panel 1: Shannon by sex (adults only)
  p_sex <- ggplot(div_combined_adults,
                  aes(x = Sex, y = Shannon, fill = Sex)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Diet Diversity by Sex (P. kingsleyae Adults)",
         x = "Sex", y = "Shannon Diversity") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  # Panel 2: Body size by sex (adults only)
  p_size_sex <- ggplot(div_combined_adults,
                       aes(x = Sex, y = body_size, fill = Sex)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Body Size by Sex (P. kingsleyae Adults)",
         x = "Sex",
         y = "Standard Length (mm)") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  # Panel 3: Shannon vs body size with sex indicated
  p_scatter <- ggplot(div_combined_all,
                      aes(x = body_size, y = Shannon, color = Sex)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Diet Diversity vs Body Size (All P. kingsleyae)",
         x = "Standard Length (mm)",
         y = "Shannon Diversity",
         color = "Sex")

  # Combine plots
  print(p_sex / p_size_sex / p_scatter)

} else {
  cat("Insufficient data for sex analysis\n")
}
```

## 3.3 Body Size Effects

Test for linear relationship between body size and diet diversity across all *P. kingsleyae* individuals.

```{r level3-body-size, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 3.3: BODY SIZE EFFECTS ===\n")
cat("Testing Shannon ~ body_size for all P. kingsleyae individuals\n\n")

# PS1
cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
lm_size_ps1 <- lm(Shannon ~ body_size, data = div_ps1_pk)
print(summary(lm_size_ps1))
cat("\nPearson correlation:\n")
cor_size_ps1 <- cor.test(div_ps1_pk$Shannon, div_ps1_pk$body_size)
cat("  r:", round(cor_size_ps1$estimate, 3), "\n")
cat("  95% CI: [", round(cor_size_ps1$conf.int[1], 3), ",",
    round(cor_size_ps1$conf.int[2], 3), "]\n")
cat("  P-value:", format.pval(cor_size_ps1$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")

# PS2
cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
lm_size_ps2 <- lm(Shannon ~ body_size, data = div_ps2_pk)
print(summary(lm_size_ps2))
cat("\nPearson correlation:\n")
cor_size_ps2 <- cor.test(div_ps2_pk$Shannon, div_ps2_pk$body_size)
cat("  r:", round(cor_size_ps2$estimate, 3), "\n")
cat("  95% CI: [", round(cor_size_ps2$conf.int[1], 3), ",",
    round(cor_size_ps2$conf.int[2], 3), "]\n")
cat("  P-value:", format.pval(cor_size_ps2$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")

# Scatterplot with regression line
if (exists("div_combined_all")) {
  p_size_effect <- ggplot(div_combined_all,
                          aes(x = body_size, y = Shannon)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = Sex), alpha = 0.6, size = 3) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",
                linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Body Size Effect on Diet Diversity (P. kingsleyae)",
         subtitle = paste0("PS1: r = ", round(cor_size_ps1$estimate, 3),
                          ", p ", format.pval(cor_size_ps1$p.value, digits = 2),
                          " | PS2: r = ", round(cor_size_ps2$estimate, 3),
                          ", p ", format.pval(cor_size_ps2$p.value, digits = 2)),
         x = "Standard Length (mm)",
         y = "Shannon Diversity",
         color = "Sex")

  print(p_size_effect)
}
```

## 3.4 Parasitism Effects

Test whether parasitism affects diet diversity and composition in *P. kingsleyae*.

```{r level3-parasitism, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
cat("\n=== LEVEL 3.4: PARASITISM EFFECTS ===\n\n")

# Check if parasitism data exists
check_parasitism <- function(ps) {
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))

  # Look for parasite-related columns
  parasite_cols <- grep("parasite|parasit", names(meta),
                        ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)

  if (length(parasite_cols) == 0) {
    cat("No parasitism columns found in metadata\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  cat("Found parasitism columns:", paste(parasite_cols, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  return(parasite_cols)
}

# Check both primer sets
cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
parasite_cols_ps1 <- check_parasitism(ps1_pk)

cat("\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
parasite_cols_ps2 <- check_parasitism(ps2_pk)

# Analyze parasitism effects if data exists
if (!is.null(parasite_cols_ps1) && length(parasite_cols_ps1) > 0) {

  # Use the first parasite column found
  parasite_col <- parasite_cols_ps1[1]
  cat("\n=== ANALYZING PARASITISM USING COLUMN:", parasite_col, "===\n\n")

  # Prepare data with parasitism
  prepare_parasite_data <- function(ps, parasite_col) {
    meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))
    div <- estimate_richness(ps, measures = c("Shannon"))

    div$Parasite <- meta[[parasite_col]]
    div$body_size <- as.numeric(meta$SL)
    div$Sample_Name <- meta$Sample_Name
    div$Sex <- meta$Sex

    # Remove missing data
    div <- div[!is.na(div$Parasite) & !is.na(div$body_size), ]

    # Standardize parasite values (yes/no, presence/absence, etc.)
    div$Parasite <- tolower(as.character(div$Parasite))
    div$Parasite <- ifelse(div$Parasite %in% c("yes", "present", "1", "true"),
                           "Yes",
                           ifelse(div$Parasite %in% c("no", "absent", "0", "false"),
                                 "No", div$Parasite))

    return(div)
  }

  div_para_ps1 <- prepare_parasite_data(ps1_pk, parasite_col)
  div_para_ps2 <- prepare_parasite_data(ps2_pk, parasite_col)

  cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
  cat("  Samples with parasite data:", nrow(div_para_ps1), "\n")
  cat("  Parasitism status:\n")
  print(table(div_para_ps1$Parasite))

  cat("\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
  cat("  Samples with parasite data:", nrow(div_para_ps2), "\n")
  cat("  Parasitism status:\n")
  print(table(div_para_ps2$Parasite))

  # Check if parasitism is categorical or continuous
  is_categorical <- length(unique(div_para_ps1$Parasite)) < 10

  if (is_categorical) {
    cat("\n=== CATEGORICAL PARASITISM ANALYSIS ===\n")

    # Kruskal-Wallis test for alpha diversity
    cat("\nAlpha Diversity (Shannon) by Parasitism Status:\n\n")

    # PS1
    cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
    kw_ps1 <- kruskal.test(Shannon ~ Parasite, data = div_para_ps1)
    print(kw_ps1)

    # Summary statistics
    para_summary_ps1 <- div_para_ps1 %>%
      group_by(Parasite) %>%
      summarise(
        n = n(),
        mean_shannon = mean(Shannon, na.rm = TRUE),
        sd_shannon = sd(Shannon, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    print(para_summary_ps1)

    # PS2
    cat("\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
    kw_ps2 <- kruskal.test(Shannon ~ Parasite, data = div_para_ps2)
    print(kw_ps2)

    para_summary_ps2 <- div_para_ps2 %>%
      group_by(Parasite) %>%
      summarise(
        n = n(),
        mean_shannon = mean(Shannon, na.rm = TRUE),
        sd_shannon = sd(Shannon, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    print(para_summary_ps2)

    # ANCOVA controlling for body size
    cat("\n=== ANCOVA: Shannon ~ Parasite + body_size ===\n\n")

    # PS1
    cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
    ancova_para_ps1 <- lm(Shannon ~ Parasite + body_size, data = div_para_ps1)
    print(summary(ancova_para_ps1))
    cat("\nANOVA table:\n")
    print(anova(ancova_para_ps1))

    # PS2
    cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
    ancova_para_ps2 <- lm(Shannon ~ Parasite + body_size, data = div_para_ps2)
    print(summary(ancova_para_ps2))
    cat("\nANOVA table:\n")
    print(anova(ancova_para_ps2))

  } else {
    cat("\n=== CONTINUOUS PARASITISM ANALYSIS ===\n")
    cat("Parasitism appears to be continuous. Using correlation/regression.\n\n")

    # Convert to numeric if possible
    div_para_ps1$Parasite_numeric <- as.numeric(div_para_ps1$Parasite)
    div_para_ps2$Parasite_numeric <- as.numeric(div_para_ps2$Parasite)

    # ANCOVA with continuous parasite measure
    cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
    ancova_para_ps1 <- lm(Shannon ~ Parasite_numeric + body_size,
                         data = div_para_ps1)
    print(summary(ancova_para_ps1))

    cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
    ancova_para_ps2 <- lm(Shannon ~ Parasite_numeric + body_size,
                         data = div_para_ps2)
    print(summary(ancova_para_ps2))
  }

  # Beta diversity analysis
  cat("\n\n=== BETA DIVERSITY: PERMANOVA ===\n")
  cat("Testing: Bray-Curtis ~ Parasitism\n\n")

  # Create phyloseq subsets with parasitism metadata
  prepare_parasite_phyloseq <- function(ps, parasite_col) {
    meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))

    # Standardize parasite column
    meta$Parasite_status <- tolower(as.character(meta[[parasite_col]]))
    meta$Parasite_status <- ifelse(
      meta$Parasite_status %in% c("yes", "present", "1", "true"),
      "Yes",
      ifelse(meta$Parasite_status %in% c("no", "absent", "0", "false"),
             "No", meta$Parasite_status)
    )

    # Update sample data
    sample_data(ps) <- meta

    # Remove samples with missing parasite data
    ps_filt <- subset_samples(ps, !is.na(Parasite_status) &
                                   Parasite_status != "")

    return(ps_filt)
  }

  ps1_pk_para <- prepare_parasite_phyloseq(ps1_pk, parasite_col)
  ps2_pk_para <- prepare_parasite_phyloseq(ps2_pk, parasite_col)

  # Run beta diversity analysis
  if (nsamples(ps1_pk_para) > 5) {
    cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
    beta_para_ps1 <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps1_pk_para, "~ Parasite_status")
    print(beta_para_ps1$permanova)

    cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
    beta_para_ps2 <- run_beta_diversity_analysis(ps2_pk_para, "~ Parasite_status")
    print(beta_para_ps2$permanova)
  } else {
    cat("Insufficient samples with parasite data for beta diversity analysis\n")
  }

  # Visualization
  div_para_ps1$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 1"
  div_para_ps2$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 2"
  div_para_combined <- bind_rows(div_para_ps1, div_para_ps2)

  # Boxplot of Shannon by parasitism status
  p_para_box <- ggplot(div_para_combined,
                       aes(x = Parasite, y = Shannon, fill = Parasite)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
    facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Diet Diversity by Parasitism Status (P. kingsleyae)",
         x = "Parasitism Status",
         y = "Shannon Diversity") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  # Scatterplot of Shannon vs body size colored by parasitism
  p_para_scatter <- ggplot(div_para_combined,
                           aes(x = body_size, y = Shannon, color = Parasite)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 3) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "Body Size, Parasitism, and Diet Diversity",
         x = "Standard Length (mm)",
         y = "Shannon Diversity",
         color = "Parasitism")

  print(p_para_box / p_para_scatter)

} else {
  cat("\nNo parasitism data available in metadata. Skipping analysis.\n")
}
```

---

# LEVEL 4: BP vs. TP Within Paramormyrops kingsleyae

**The Core Question: Does EOD waveform associate with dietary niche partitioning?**

**Design consideration:** Waveform is confounded with geography in this dataset. BP waveforms are found in southern rivers (Bambomo, Apassa), while TP waveforms are found in northern rivers (Mengono, Iboundji). This level implements multiple analytical approaches to separate geographic effects from waveform effects.

**Analysis strategy:**
1. Two-way ANOVA/PERMANOVA (Region × Waveform) to assess relative contributions
2. Variance partitioning to quantify pure vs. shared effects
3. Waveform similarity across sites to test whether same-waveform fish in different regions are more similar than different-waveform fish in the same region
4. Prey selectivity analysis to distinguish composition differences from selection behavior

## 4.1 Prey Composition: BP vs. TP Emphasis

This section reuses the 4-panel visualization from 3.1 but emphasizes the BP vs. TP comparison within each region.

```{r level4-composition-emphasis, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
cat("=== LEVEL 4.1: PREY COMPOSITION - BP vs. TP EMPHASIS ===\n\n")

# The 4-panel plot from 3.1 already shows Region × Waveform organization
# Here we add specific statistical comparisons

# Calculate mean composition similarity within groups
calc_group_similarity <- function(ps) {
  # Get OTU table
  otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps))
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
    otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
  }

  # Calculate Bray-Curtis distance
  dist_mat <- vegdist(otu_mat, method = "bray")

  # Get metadata
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))
  meta$Group <- paste(meta$Region, meta$Waveform_Type, sep = "_")

  # Calculate within-group vs. between-group similarities
  dist_df <- as.matrix(dist_mat) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("Sample1") %>%
    pivot_longer(-Sample1, names_to = "Sample2", values_to = "Distance") %>%
    filter(Sample1 < Sample2)  # Remove duplicates and self-comparisons

  # Add group info
  dist_df$Group1 <- meta$Group[match(dist_df$Sample1, rownames(meta))]
  dist_df$Group2 <- meta$Group[match(dist_df$Sample2, rownames(meta))]
  dist_df$Region1 <- meta$Region[match(dist_df$Sample1, rownames(meta))]
  dist_df$Region2 <- meta$Region[match(dist_df$Sample2, rownames(meta))]
  dist_df$Waveform1 <- meta$Waveform_Type[match(dist_df$Sample1, rownames(meta))]
  dist_df$Waveform2 <- meta$Waveform_Type[match(dist_df$Sample2, rownames(meta))]

  # Categorize comparisons
  dist_df <- dist_df %>%
    mutate(
      Comparison = case_when(
        Group1 == Group2 ~ "Same Region, Same Waveform",
        Region1 == Region2 & Waveform1 != Waveform2 ~ "Same Region, Different Waveform",
        Region1 != Region2 & Waveform1 == Waveform2 ~ "Different Region, Same Waveform",
        Region1 != Region2 & Waveform1 != Waveform2 ~ "Different Region, Different Waveform",
        TRUE ~ "Other"
      )
    )

  return(dist_df)
}

# Calculate for both primer sets
dist_comp_ps1 <- calc_group_similarity(ps1_pk)
dist_comp_ps2 <- calc_group_similarity(ps2_pk)

# Summary statistics
cat("PRIMERSET 1 - Mean Bray-Curtis distances:\n")
dist_summary_ps1 <- dist_comp_ps1 %>%
  group_by(Comparison) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_dist = mean(Distance),
    sd_dist = sd(Distance),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_dist)

print(dist_summary_ps1)

cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2 - Mean Bray-Curtis distances:\n")
dist_summary_ps2 <- dist_comp_ps2 %>%
  group_by(Comparison) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_dist = mean(Distance),
    sd_dist = sd(Distance),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_dist)

print(dist_summary_ps2)

# Visualization
dist_comp_ps1$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 1"
dist_comp_ps2$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 2"
dist_comp_combined <- bind_rows(dist_comp_ps1, dist_comp_ps2)

# Reorder comparison levels for logical display
dist_comp_combined$Comparison <- factor(
  dist_comp_combined$Comparison,
  levels = c("Same Region, Same Waveform",
             "Same Region, Different Waveform",
             "Different Region, Same Waveform",
             "Different Region, Different Waveform")
)

p_comp_distances <- ggplot(dist_comp_combined,
                            aes(x = Comparison, y = Distance, fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "P. kingsleyae: Dietary Dissimilarity by Region and Waveform",
    subtitle = "Lower values = more similar diets",
    x = "Comparison Type",
    y = "Bray-Curtis Distance"
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d()

print(p_comp_distances)

# Statistical test: Are different waveforms in same region more different than
# same waveforms in different regions?
cat("\n=== KEY STATISTICAL TEST ===\n")
cat("Question: Do waveforms predict diet better than geography?\n")
cat("Test: Same Waveform/Different Region vs. Different Waveform/Same Region\n\n")

test_ps1 <- dist_comp_ps1 %>%
  filter(Comparison %in% c("Same Region, Different Waveform",
                          "Different Region, Same Waveform"))

if (nrow(test_ps1) > 0) {
  cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
  wt_ps1 <- wilcox.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = test_ps1)
  print(wt_ps1)

  cat("\nInterpretation:\n")
  if (wt_ps1$p.value < 0.05) {
    mean_same_wave <- mean(test_ps1$Distance[test_ps1$Comparison == "Different Region, Same Waveform"])
    mean_diff_wave <- mean(test_ps1$Distance[test_ps1$Comparison == "Same Region, Different Waveform"])

    if (mean_same_wave < mean_diff_wave) {
      cat("Same waveform in different regions are MORE similar than\n")
      cat("different waveforms in same region (p < 0.05).\n")
      cat("→ WAVEFORM predicts diet better than GEOGRAPHY\n")
    } else {
      cat("Different waveforms in same region are MORE similar than\n")
      cat("same waveform in different regions (p < 0.05).\n")
      cat("→ GEOGRAPHY predicts diet better than WAVEFORM\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("No significant difference (p >= 0.05).\n")
    cat("Cannot distinguish waveform from geographic effects.\n")
  }
}

test_ps2 <- dist_comp_ps2 %>%
  filter(Comparison %in% c("Same Region, Different Waveform",
                          "Different Region, Same Waveform"))

if (nrow(test_ps2) > 0) {
  cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
  wt_ps2 <- wilcox.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = test_ps2)
  print(wt_ps2)

  cat("\nInterpretation:\n")
  if (wt_ps2$p.value < 0.05) {
    mean_same_wave <- mean(test_ps2$Distance[test_ps2$Comparison == "Different Region, Same Waveform"])
    mean_diff_wave <- mean(test_ps2$Distance[test_ps2$Comparison == "Same Region, Different Waveform"])

    if (mean_same_wave < mean_diff_wave) {
      cat("Same waveform in different regions are MORE similar than\n")
      cat("different waveforms in same region (p < 0.05).\n")
      cat("→ WAVEFORM predicts diet better than GEOGRAPHY\n")
    } else {
      cat("Different waveforms in same region are MORE similar than\n")
      cat("same waveform in different regions (p < 0.05).\n")
      cat("→ GEOGRAPHY predicts diet better than WAVEFORM\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("No significant difference (p >= 0.05).\n")
    cat("Cannot distinguish waveform from geographic effects.\n")
  }
}
```

## 4.2 Alpha Diversity: Two-Way ANOVA

Test whether Shannon diversity differs by Region and/or Waveform using two-way ANOVA with interaction. This approach allows us to assess:

1. Main effect of Region
2. Main effect of Waveform
3. Region × Waveform interaction
4. Marginal effects (Type II/III SS approach)

```{r level4-alpha-2way, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
cat("=== LEVEL 4.2: ALPHA DIVERSITY - TWO-WAY ANOVA ===\n\n")

# Calculate diversity metrics
div1_pk <- estimate_richness(ps1_pk, measures = c("Shannon"))
meta1_pk <- data.frame(sample_data(ps1_pk), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Add grouping variables
div1_pk$Region <- ifelse(meta1_pk$Locality_Name %in% c("Mengono Creek", "Iboundji Creek"),
                        "North", "South")
div1_pk$Waveform_Type <- meta1_pk$Waveform_Type
div1_pk$Locality_Name <- meta1_pk$Locality_Name
div1_pk$Sample_Name <- meta1_pk$Sample_Name

# Convert to factors
div1_pk$Region <- factor(div1_pk$Region, levels = c("North", "South"))
div1_pk$Waveform_Type <- factor(div1_pk$Waveform_Type)

# Same for PS2
div2_pk <- estimate_richness(ps2_pk, measures = c("Shannon"))
meta2_pk <- data.frame(sample_data(ps2_pk), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

div2_pk$Region <- ifelse(meta2_pk$Locality_Name %in% c("Mengono Creek", "Iboundji Creek"),
                        "North", "South")
div2_pk$Waveform_Type <- meta2_pk$Waveform_Type
div2_pk$Locality_Name <- meta2_pk$Locality_Name
div2_pk$Sample_Name <- meta2_pk$Sample_Name

div2_pk$Region <- factor(div2_pk$Region, levels = c("North", "South"))
div2_pk$Waveform_Type <- factor(div2_pk$Waveform_Type)

# PRIMERSET 1 Analysis
cat("=== PRIMERSET 1 ===\n\n")

# Interactive model (Region × Waveform)
cat("Two-Way ANOVA with Interaction (Region × Waveform):\n")
anova_pk1_int <- aov(Shannon ~ Region * Waveform_Type, data = div1_pk)
print(summary(anova_pk1_int))

# Additive model (Region + Waveform)
cat("\n\nAdditive Model (Region + Waveform):\n")
anova_pk1_add <- aov(Shannon ~ Region + Waveform_Type, data = div1_pk)
print(summary(anova_pk1_add))

# Marginal effects using Type II SS
cat("\n\nMarginal Effects (Type II Sums of Squares):\n")
if (requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE)) {
  print(car::Anova(anova_pk1_add, type = "II"))
} else {
  cat("car package not available. Showing sequential tests:\n\n")

  cat("Region only:\n")
  anova_pk1_region <- aov(Shannon ~ Region, data = div1_pk)
  print(summary(anova_pk1_region))

  cat("\nWaveform only:\n")
  anova_pk1_wave <- aov(Shannon ~ Waveform_Type, data = div1_pk)
  print(summary(anova_pk1_wave))
}

# Summary statistics by group
cat("\n\nDescriptive Statistics:\n")
div1_pk %>%
  group_by(Region, Waveform_Type) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_shannon = mean(Shannon),
    sd_shannon = sd(Shannon),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))

# PRIMERSET 2 Analysis
cat("\n\n=== PRIMERSET 2 ===\n\n")

# Interactive model
cat("Two-Way ANOVA with Interaction (Region × Waveform):\n")
anova_pk2_int <- aov(Shannon ~ Region * Waveform_Type, data = div2_pk)
print(summary(anova_pk2_int))

# Additive model
cat("\n\nAdditive Model (Region + Waveform):\n")
anova_pk2_add <- aov(Shannon ~ Region + Waveform_Type, data = div2_pk)
print(summary(anova_pk2_add))

# Marginal effects
cat("\n\nMarginal Effects (Type II Sums of Squares):\n")
if (requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE)) {
  print(car::Anova(anova_pk2_add, type = "II"))
} else {
  cat("car package not available. Showing sequential tests:\n\n")

  cat("Region only:\n")
  anova_pk2_region <- aov(Shannon ~ Region, data = div2_pk)
  print(summary(anova_pk2_region))

  cat("\nWaveform only:\n")
  anova_pk2_wave <- aov(Shannon ~ Waveform_Type, data = div2_pk)
  print(summary(anova_pk2_wave))
}

# Summary statistics
cat("\n\nDescriptive Statistics:\n")
div2_pk %>%
  group_by(Region, Waveform_Type) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_shannon = mean(Shannon),
    sd_shannon = sd(Shannon),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 3, booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))

# Visualization: Interaction plot
div1_pk$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 1"
div2_pk$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 2"
div_combined <- bind_rows(div1_pk, div2_pk)

p_alpha_interact <- ggplot(div_combined,
                           aes(x = Region, y = Shannon, color = Waveform_Type,
                               group = Waveform_Type)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "P. kingsleyae: Shannon Diversity by Region and Waveform",
    subtitle = "Interaction plot with mean ± SE",
    x = "Region",
    y = "Shannon Diversity",
    color = "Waveform"
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1")

print(p_alpha_interact)

# Boxplot version
p_alpha_box <- ggplot(div_combined,
                      aes(x = Region, y = Shannon, fill = Waveform_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "P. kingsleyae: Shannon Diversity Distribution",
    x = "Region",
    y = "Shannon Diversity",
    fill = "Waveform"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1")

print(p_alpha_box)

cat("\n=== RESULT INTERPRETATION ===\n")
cat("Expected pattern if waveform matters: Significant Waveform main effect\n")
cat("Expected pattern if confounded: Significant Region effect, NS Waveform effect\n")
```

## 4.3 Beta Diversity: Two-Way PERMANOVA

Apply PERMANOVA to test Region and Waveform effects on diet composition (Bray-Curtis dissimilarity). Use marginal tests (by="margin") to assess independent contributions.

```{r level4-beta-2way, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
cat("=== LEVEL 4.3: BETA DIVERSITY - TWO-WAY PERMANOVA ===\n\n")

# Prepare data for PERMANOVA - PS1
otu1_pk <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps1_pk))
if (taxa_are_rows(ps1_pk)) {
  otu1_pk <- t(otu1_pk)
}

meta1_pk_beta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps1_pk), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
meta1_pk_beta$Region <- factor(
  ifelse(meta1_pk_beta$Locality_Name %in% c("Mengono Creek", "Iboundji Creek"),
         "North", "South"),
  levels = c("North", "South")
)
meta1_pk_beta$Waveform_Type <- factor(meta1_pk_beta$Waveform_Type)

# Ensure rownames match
rownames(meta1_pk_beta) <- rownames(otu1_pk)

dist1_pk <- vegdist(otu1_pk, method = "bray")

# Prepare data for PERMANOVA - PS2
otu2_pk <- as.matrix(otu_table(ps2_pk))
if (taxa_are_rows(ps2_pk)) {
  otu2_pk <- t(otu2_pk)
}

meta2_pk_beta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps2_pk), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
meta2_pk_beta$Region <- factor(
  ifelse(meta2_pk_beta$Locality_Name %in% c("Mengono Creek", "Iboundji Creek"),
         "North", "South"),
  levels = c("North", "South")
)
meta2_pk_beta$Waveform_Type <- factor(meta2_pk_beta$Waveform_Type)

rownames(meta2_pk_beta) <- rownames(otu2_pk)

dist2_pk <- vegdist(otu2_pk, method = "bray")

# PRIMERSET 1 - PERMANOVA
cat("=== PRIMERSET 1 ===\n\n")

# Additive model with sequential testing
cat("Additive Model - Sequential (Region first):\n")
perm_pk1_seq_region <- adonis2(dist1_pk ~ Region + Waveform_Type,
                               data = meta1_pk_beta,
                               permutations = 999,
                               by = "terms")
print(perm_pk1_seq_region)

cat("\n\nAdditive Model - Sequential (Waveform first):\n")
perm_pk1_seq_wave <- adonis2(dist1_pk ~ Waveform_Type + Region,
                             data = meta1_pk_beta,
                             permutations = 999,
                             by = "terms")
print(perm_pk1_seq_wave)

# Marginal effects (most appropriate for confounded variables)
cat("\n\nAdditive Model - Marginal Effects (Type III):\n")
perm_pk1_margin <- adonis2(dist1_pk ~ Region + Waveform_Type,
                           data = meta1_pk_beta,
                           permutations = 999,
                           by = "margin")
print(perm_pk1_margin)

# Interactive model
cat("\n\nInteractive Model (Region × Waveform):\n")
perm_pk1_int <- adonis2(dist1_pk ~ Region * Waveform_Type,
                        data = meta1_pk_beta,
                        permutations = 999)
print(perm_pk1_int)

# Extract R² values for reporting
r2_region_ps1 <- perm_pk1_margin$R2[1]
r2_wave_ps1 <- perm_pk1_margin$R2[2]
p_region_ps1 <- perm_pk1_margin$`Pr(>F)`[1]
p_wave_ps1 <- perm_pk1_margin$`Pr(>F)`[2]

cat("\n\nEffect Size Summary (Marginal R²):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Region: R² = %.3f, p = %.3f\n", r2_region_ps1, p_region_ps1))
cat(sprintf("  Waveform: R² = %.3f, p = %.3f\n", r2_wave_ps1, p_wave_ps1))

# PRIMERSET 2 - PERMANOVA
cat("\n\n=== PRIMERSET 2 ===\n\n")

cat("Additive Model - Sequential (Region first):\n")
perm_pk2_seq_region <- adonis2(dist2_pk ~ Region + Waveform_Type,
                               data = meta2_pk_beta,
                               permutations = 999,
                               by = "terms")
print(perm_pk2_seq_region)

cat("\n\nAdditive Model - Sequential (Waveform first):\n")
perm_pk2_seq_wave <- adonis2(dist2_pk ~ Waveform_Type + Region,
                             data = meta2_pk_beta,
                             permutations = 999,
                             by = "terms")
print(perm_pk2_seq_wave)

cat("\n\nAdditive Model - Marginal Effects (Type III):\n")
perm_pk2_margin <- adonis2(dist2_pk ~ Region + Waveform_Type,
                           data = meta2_pk_beta,
                           permutations = 999,
                           by = "margin")
print(perm_pk2_margin)

cat("\n\nInteractive Model (Region × Waveform):\n")
perm_pk2_int <- adonis2(dist2_pk ~ Region * Waveform_Type,
                        data = meta2_pk_beta,
                        permutations = 999)
print(perm_pk2_int)

# Extract R² values
r2_region_ps2 <- perm_pk2_margin$R2[1]
r2_wave_ps2 <- perm_pk2_margin$R2[2]
p_region_ps2 <- perm_pk2_margin$`Pr(>F)`[1]
p_wave_ps2 <- perm_pk2_margin$`Pr(>F)`[2]

cat("\n\nEffect Size Summary (Marginal R²):\n")
cat(sprintf("  Region: R² = %.3f, p = %.3f\n", r2_region_ps2, p_region_ps2))
cat(sprintf("  Waveform: R² = %.3f, p = %.3f\n", r2_wave_ps2, p_wave_ps2))

# PCoA visualization
pcoa1_pk <- cmdscale(dist1_pk, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
var_exp1_pk <- pcoa1_pk$eig / sum(pcoa1_pk$eig) * 100

pcoa2_pk <- cmdscale(dist2_pk, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
var_exp2_pk <- pcoa2_pk$eig / sum(pcoa2_pk$eig) * 100

# Create PCoA data frames
pcoa1_df <- data.frame(
  PCoA1 = pcoa1_pk$points[, 1],
  PCoA2 = pcoa1_pk$points[, 2],
  meta1_pk_beta,
  PrimerSet = "Primerset 1"
)

pcoa2_df <- data.frame(
  PCoA1 = pcoa2_pk$points[, 1],
  PCoA2 = pcoa2_pk$points[, 2],
  meta2_pk_beta,
  PrimerSet = "Primerset 2"
)

pcoa_combined <- bind_rows(pcoa1_df, pcoa2_df)

# Plot: Color by Region, Shape by Waveform
p_pcoa_region_wave <- ggplot(pcoa_combined,
                              aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2,
                                  color = Region, shape = Waveform_Type)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "P. kingsleyae: Diet Composition by Region and Waveform",
    subtitle = "PCoA on Bray-Curtis dissimilarity",
    color = "Region",
    shape = "Waveform"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

print(p_pcoa_region_wave)

# Alternative: Color by Waveform, Shape by Region
p_pcoa_wave_region <- ggplot(pcoa_combined,
                              aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2,
                                  color = Waveform_Type, shape = Region)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  labs(
    title = "P. kingsleyae: Diet Composition (Waveform Emphasis)",
    subtitle = "PCoA on Bray-Curtis dissimilarity",
    color = "Waveform",
    shape = "Region"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

print(p_pcoa_wave_region)

cat("\n=== RESULT INTERPRETATION ===\n")
cat("Marginal R² values indicate independent contribution of each factor\n")
cat("after accounting for the other.\n")
cat("If Waveform R² > 0.10 and significant: suggests dietary niche partitioning\n")
cat("If Region R² >> Waveform R²: geographic effects dominate\n")
```

## 4.4 Variance Partitioning

Decompose variation in diet composition into pure Region effect, pure Waveform effect, and shared (confounded) variation using variance partitioning.

```{r level4-varpart, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
cat("=== LEVEL 4.4: VARIANCE PARTITIONING ===\n\n")

# This analysis partitions the variation explained by Region and Waveform
# into pure and shared components

# Prepare beta diversity results for variance partitioning
beta1_pk <- list(
  distance = dist1_pk,
  metadata = meta1_pk_beta
)

beta2_pk <- list(
  distance = dist2_pk,
  metadata = meta2_pk_beta
)

# Run variance partitioning (uses helper function from gut_analysis_2026_helpers.R)
cat("=== PRIMERSET 1 ===\n\n")
varpart_pk1 <- variance_partition_permanova(
  beta1_pk,
  var1_formula = "~ Region",
  var2_formula = "~ Waveform_Type"
)

cat("Variance Partitioning Results:\n")
print(varpart_pk1$varpart)

cat("\n\nSequential PERMANOVA (Region first):\n")
print(varpart_pk1$sequential_var1_first)

cat("\n\nSequential PERMANOVA (Waveform first):\n")
print(varpart_pk1$sequential_var2_first)

cat("\n\n#### Marginal PERMANOVA:\n\n")
kable(as.data.frame(varpart_pk1$marginal),
            digits = 3,
            booktabs = TRUE,
            caption = "Marginal PERMANOVA: Primerset 1") %>%
      kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position"),
                    font_size = 9,
                    full_width = FALSE)

# Extract variance components
# varpart returns: [a] = pure Region, [b] = pure Waveform, [c] = shared
vp1 <- varpart_pk1$varpart
if (!is.null(vp1$part$indfract)) {
  pure_region_ps1 <- vp1$part$indfract$Adj.R.squared[1]
  pure_wave_ps1 <- vp1$part$indfract$Adj.R.squared[2]
  shared_ps1 <- vp1$part$indfract$Adj.R.squared[3]

  cat("\n\nVariance Components (Adjusted R²):\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Pure Region: %.4f\n", pure_region_ps1))
  cat(sprintf("  Pure Waveform: %.4f\n", pure_wave_ps1))
  cat(sprintf("  Shared (confounded): %.4f\n", shared_ps1))
  cat(sprintf("  Total explained: %.4f\n",
              pure_region_ps1 + pure_wave_ps1 + shared_ps1))
}

cat("\n\n=== PRIMERSET 2 ===\n\n")
varpart_pk2 <- variance_partition_permanova(
  beta2_pk,
  var1_formula = "~ Region",
  var2_formula = "~ Waveform_Type"
)

cat("Variance Partitioning Results:\n")
print(varpart_pk2$varpart)

cat("\n\nSequential PERMANOVA (Region first):\n")
print(varpart_pk2$sequential_var1_first)

cat("\n\nSequential PERMANOVA (Waveform first):\n")
print(varpart_pk2$sequential_var2_first)

cat("\n\n#### Marginal PERMANOVA:\n\n")
kable(as.data.frame(varpart_pk2$marginal),
            digits = 3,
            booktabs = TRUE,
            caption = "Marginal PERMANOVA: Primerset 2") %>%
      kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position"),
                    font_size = 9,
                    full_width = FALSE)

vp2 <- varpart_pk2$varpart
if (!is.null(vp2$part$indfract)) {
  pure_region_ps2 <- vp2$part$indfract$Adj.R.squared[1]
  pure_wave_ps2 <- vp2$part$indfract$Adj.R.squared[2]
  shared_ps2 <- vp2$part$indfract$Adj.R.squared[3]

  cat("\n\nVariance Components (Adjusted R²):\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Pure Region: %.4f\n", pure_region_ps2))
  cat(sprintf("  Pure Waveform: %.4f\n", pure_wave_ps2))
  cat(sprintf("  Shared (confounded): %.4f\n", shared_ps2))
  cat(sprintf("  Total explained: %.4f\n",
              pure_region_ps2 + pure_wave_ps2 + shared_ps2))
}

# Create summary visualization
if (!is.null(vp1$part$indfract) && !is.null(vp2$part$indfract)) {

  varpart_summary <- data.frame(
    PrimerSet = rep(c("PS1", "PS2"), each = 3),
    Component = rep(c("Pure Region", "Pure Waveform", "Shared"), 2),
    R2 = c(pure_region_ps1, pure_wave_ps1, shared_ps1,
           pure_region_ps2, pure_wave_ps2, shared_ps2)
  )

  # Handle negative values (set to 0 for visualization)
  varpart_summary$R2_plot <- pmax(varpart_summary$R2, 0)

  p_varpart <- ggplot(varpart_summary,
                      aes(x = PrimerSet, y = R2_plot, fill = Component)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    labs(
      title = "Variance Partitioning: Region vs. Waveform",
      subtitle = "Decomposition of explained variation in diet composition",
      x = "Primer Set",
      y = "Proportion of Variance (Adjusted R²)",
      fill = "Component"
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = varpart_summary %>%
        group_by(PrimerSet) %>%
        mutate(
          pos = cumsum(R2_plot) - 0.5 * R2_plot,
          label = sprintf("%.3f", R2)
        ),
      aes(y = pos, label = label),
      size = 3
    )

  print(p_varpart)
}

cat("\n=== INTERPRETATION ===\n")
cat("Pure Waveform variance indicates effect independent of geography.\n")
cat("Large Shared variance indicates confounding between Region and Waveform.\n")
cat("If Pure Waveform > 0.05: suggests waveform has independent effect on diet.\n")
```

## 4.5 Waveform Similarity Across Sites - CRITICAL TEST

This is the key test to determine whether waveform predicts diet better than geography. Compare dietary similarity of:
- Same waveform in different regions vs.
- Different waveforms in same region

If same-waveform-different-region < different-waveform-same-region → waveform matters more than geography.

```{r level4-waveform-similarity, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
cat("=== LEVEL 4.5: WAVEFORM SIMILARITY ACROSS SITES ===\n")
cat("THE CRITICAL TEST FOR WAVEFORM EFFECT\n\n")

# This analysis was already performed in section 4.1
# Here we formalize it as the key statistical test

# Extract pairwise distances and categorize
analyze_waveform_geography <- function(dist_mat, metadata) {

  # Convert distance matrix to data frame
  dist_df <- as.matrix(dist_mat) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("Sample1") %>%
    pivot_longer(-Sample1, names_to = "Sample2", values_to = "Distance") %>%
    filter(Sample1 < Sample2)  # Remove self and duplicate comparisons

  # Add metadata
  dist_df$Region1 <- metadata$Region[match(dist_df$Sample1, rownames(metadata))]
  dist_df$Region2 <- metadata$Region[match(dist_df$Sample2, rownames(metadata))]
  dist_df$Waveform1 <- metadata$Waveform_Type[match(dist_df$Sample1, rownames(metadata))]
  dist_df$Waveform2 <- metadata$Waveform_Type[match(dist_df$Sample2, rownames(metadata))]

  # Create comparison categories
  dist_df <- dist_df %>%
    mutate(
      Same_Region = Region1 == Region2,
      Same_Waveform = Waveform1 == Waveform2,
      Comparison = case_when(
        Same_Region & Same_Waveform ~ "Same Region, Same Waveform",
        Same_Region & !Same_Waveform ~ "Same Region, Different Waveform",
        !Same_Region & Same_Waveform ~ "Different Region, Same Waveform",
        !Same_Region & !Same_Waveform ~ "Different Region, Different Waveform"
      )
    )

  return(dist_df)
}

# Analyze both primer sets
dist_analysis_ps1 <- analyze_waveform_geography(dist1_pk, meta1_pk_beta)
dist_analysis_ps2 <- analyze_waveform_geography(dist2_pk, meta2_pk_beta)

# KEY STATISTICAL TEST
cat("=== THE KEY COMPARISON ===\n")
cat("H0: Same waveform in different regions has same dietary similarity as\n")
cat("    different waveforms in same region\n")
cat("HA: These groups differ in dietary similarity\n\n")

# Extract the two key comparison groups for PS1
key_comp_ps1 <- dist_analysis_ps1 %>%
  filter(Comparison %in% c("Same Region, Different Waveform",
                          "Different Region, Same Waveform"))

if (nrow(key_comp_ps1) > 0) {
  cat("=== PRIMERSET 1 ===\n\n")

  # Summary statistics
  key_summary_ps1 <- key_comp_ps1 %>%
    group_by(Comparison) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_dist = mean(Distance),
      median_dist = median(Distance),
      sd_dist = sd(Distance),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  print(key_summary_ps1)

  # Statistical test (Wilcoxon rank-sum, non-parametric)
  cat("\n\nWilcoxon Rank-Sum Test:\n")
  wt_key_ps1 <- wilcox.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = key_comp_ps1)
  print(wt_key_ps1)

  # Effect size (rank-biserial correlation)
  if (requireNamespace("rcompanion", quietly = TRUE)) {
    eff_ps1 <- rcompanion::wilcoxonR(x = key_comp_ps1$Distance,
                                    g = key_comp_ps1$Comparison)
    cat("\nEffect size (rank-biserial r):", round(eff_ps1, 3), "\n")
  }

  # Interpretation
  cat("\n\nINTERPRETATION:\n")
  mean_same_wave_ps1 <- key_summary_ps1$mean_dist[
    key_summary_ps1$Comparison == "Different Region, Same Waveform"
  ]
  mean_diff_wave_ps1 <- key_summary_ps1$mean_dist[
    key_summary_ps1$Comparison == "Same Region, Different Waveform"
  ]

  if (wt_key_ps1$p.value < 0.05) {
    if (mean_same_wave_ps1 < mean_diff_wave_ps1) {
      cat("RESULT: Fish with same waveform in different regions have MORE\n")
      cat("        similar diets than fish with different waveforms in the\n")
      cat("        same region (p < 0.05).\n\n")
      cat("CONCLUSION: WAVEFORM predicts diet better than GEOGRAPHY.\n")
      cat("            This supports dietary niche partitioning by waveform.\n")
    } else {
      cat("RESULT: Fish in same region have MORE similar diets than fish\n")
      cat("        with same waveform in different regions (p < 0.05).\n\n")
      cat("CONCLUSION: GEOGRAPHY predicts diet better than WAVEFORM.\n")
      cat("            Dietary differences are driven by site-level factors.\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("RESULT: No significant difference (p = ",
        format.pval(wt_key_ps1$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n\n")
    cat("CONCLUSION: Cannot distinguish waveform effect from geographic effect.\n")
    cat("            The confounding is too strong to separate these factors.\n")
  }
}

# Repeat for PS2
key_comp_ps2 <- dist_analysis_ps2 %>%
  filter(Comparison %in% c("Same Region, Different Waveform",
                          "Different Region, Same Waveform"))

if (nrow(key_comp_ps2) > 0) {
  cat("\n\n=== PRIMERSET 2 ===\n\n")

  key_summary_ps2 <- key_comp_ps2 %>%
    group_by(Comparison) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_dist = mean(Distance),
      median_dist = median(Distance),
      sd_dist = sd(Distance),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  print(key_summary_ps2)

  cat("\n\nWilcoxon Rank-Sum Test:\n")
  wt_key_ps2 <- wilcox.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = key_comp_ps2)
  print(wt_key_ps2)

  if (requireNamespace("rcompanion", quietly = TRUE)) {
    eff_ps2 <- rcompanion::wilcoxonR(x = key_comp_ps2$Distance,
                                    g = key_comp_ps2$Comparison)
    cat("\nEffect size (rank-biserial r):", round(eff_ps2, 3), "\n")
  }

  cat("\n\nINTERPRETATION:\n")
  mean_same_wave_ps2 <- key_summary_ps2$mean_dist[
    key_summary_ps2$Comparison == "Different Region, Same Waveform"
  ]
  mean_diff_wave_ps2 <- key_summary_ps2$mean_dist[
    key_summary_ps2$Comparison == "Same Region, Different Waveform"
  ]

  if (wt_key_ps2$p.value < 0.05) {
    if (mean_same_wave_ps2 < mean_diff_wave_ps2) {
      cat("RESULT: Fish with same waveform in different regions have MORE\n")
      cat("        similar diets than fish with different waveforms in the\n")
      cat("        same region (p < 0.05).\n\n")
      cat("CONCLUSION: WAVEFORM predicts diet better than GEOGRAPHY.\n")
      cat("            This supports dietary niche partitioning by waveform.\n")
    } else {
      cat("RESULT: Fish in same region have MORE similar diets than fish\n")
      cat("        with same waveform in different regions (p < 0.05).\n\n")
      cat("CONCLUSION: GEOGRAPHY predicts diet better than WAVEFORM.\n")
      cat("            Dietary differences are driven by site-level factors.\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("RESULT: No significant difference (p = ",
        format.pval(wt_key_ps2$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n\n")
    cat("CONCLUSION: Cannot distinguish waveform effect from geographic effect.\n")
    cat("            The confounding is too strong to separate these factors.\n")
  }
}

# Visualization
dist_analysis_ps1$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 1"
dist_analysis_ps2$PrimerSet <- "Primerset 2"
dist_combined_analysis <- bind_rows(dist_analysis_ps1, dist_analysis_ps2)

# Reorder for clarity
dist_combined_analysis$Comparison <- factor(
  dist_combined_analysis$Comparison,
  levels = c("Same Region, Same Waveform",
             "Same Region, Different Waveform",
             "Different Region, Same Waveform",
             "Different Region, Different Waveform")
)

p_key_test <- ggplot(
  dist_combined_analysis %>%
    filter(Comparison %in% c("Same Region, Different Waveform",
                            "Different Region, Same Waveform")),
  aes(x = Comparison, y = Distance, fill = Comparison)
) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "THE KEY TEST: Waveform vs. Geography",
    subtitle = "Lower distance = more similar diets",
    x = "",
    y = "Bray-Curtis Distance"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")

print(p_key_test)

# All four comparison categories
p_all_comparisons <- ggplot(dist_combined_analysis,
                            aes(x = Comparison, y = Distance, fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ PrimerSet) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "All Pairwise Comparisons: Region × Waveform",
    x = "",
    y = "Bray-Curtis Distance"
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d()

print(p_all_comparisons)

# Statistical test across all four categories
cat("\n\n=== OVERALL COMPARISON (All Four Categories) ===\n\n")

cat("PRIMERSET 1:\n")
kw_all_ps1 <- kruskal.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = dist_analysis_ps1)
print(kw_all_ps1)

if (kw_all_ps1$p.value < 0.05 && requireNamespace("FSA", quietly = TRUE)) {
  cat("\n#### Post-hoc pairwise comparisons (Dunn test):\n\n")
  dunn_ps1 <- FSA::dunnTest(Distance ~ Comparison, data = dist_analysis_ps1,
                           method = "bonferroni")
  # Abbreviate comparison names for table width
  dunn_df <- as.data.frame(dunn_ps1$res)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Different Region, Different Waveform", "DR-DW", dunn_df$Comparison)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Different Region, Same Waveform", "DR-SW", dunn_df$Comparison)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Same Region, Different Waveform", "SR-DW", dunn_df$Comparison)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Same Region, Same Waveform", "SR-SW", dunn_df$Comparison)

  cat("Abbreviations: DR=Different Region, SR=Same Region, DW=Different Waveform, SW=Same Waveform\n\n")

  kable(dunn_df, digits = 4, booktabs = TRUE,
        caption = "Dunn Test: Primerset 1") %>%
    kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position", "scale_down"),
                  full_width = FALSE, font_size = 9)
}

cat("\n\nPRIMERSET 2:\n")
kw_all_ps2 <- kruskal.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = dist_analysis_ps2)
print(kw_all_ps2)

if (kw_all_ps2$p.value < 0.05 && requireNamespace("FSA", quietly = TRUE)) {
  cat("\n#### Post-hoc pairwise comparisons (Dunn test):\n\n")
  dunn_ps2 <- FSA::dunnTest(Distance ~ Comparison, data = dist_analysis_ps2,
                           method = "bonferroni")
  # Abbreviate comparison names for table width
  dunn_df <- as.data.frame(dunn_ps2$res)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Different Region, Different Waveform", "DR-DW", dunn_df$Comparison)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Different Region, Same Waveform", "DR-SW", dunn_df$Comparison)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Same Region, Different Waveform", "SR-DW", dunn_df$Comparison)
  dunn_df$Comparison <- gsub("Same Region, Same Waveform", "SR-SW", dunn_df$Comparison)

  cat("Abbreviations: DR=Different Region, SR=Same Region, DW=Different Waveform, SW=Same Waveform\n\n")

  kable(dunn_df, digits = 4, booktabs = TRUE,
        caption = "Dunn Test: Primerset 2") %>%
    kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "HOLD_position", "scale_down"),
                  full_width = FALSE, font_size = 9)
}
```

## 4.6 Prey Selectivity Analysis

Integrate environmental prey availability data to distinguish between:
1. **Compositional differences**: BP and TP eat different prey
2. **Selectivity differences**: BP and TP show different preferences for the same available prey

This requires D-frame net data from the field sites. If available, calculate Ivlev's electivity index (E) and test whether selectivity profiles differ between waveforms.

```{r level4-selectivity, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
cat("=== LEVEL 4.6: PREY SELECTIVITY ANALYSIS ===\n\n")

# Check if environmental data is available
env_file <- file.path(root, "input_data", "gabon_inverts_compiled_cleaned_2026_final.csv")

if (file.exists(env_file)) {

  cat("Environmental prey availability data found.\n")
  cat("Loading and processing...\n\n")

  # Load environmental data
  env_raw <- read.csv(env_file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  colnames(env_raw) <- trimws(colnames(env_raw))

  # Clean and aggregate environmental data
  env_data <- env_raw %>%
    mutate(Count = as.numeric(Count)) %>%
    mutate(Family_clean = sapply(Family, clean_family_name)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Family_clean), !is.na(Count), Count > 0) %>%
    select(Site = Name, Family = Family_clean, Count)

  # Map locality names to site names in environmental data
  # This mapping depends on your data - adjust as needed
  locality_to_site <- c(
    "Bambomo" = "Bambomo Creek",
    "Apassa" = "Ipassa Creek",
    "Mengono" = "Mengono Creek",
    "Iboundji" = "Iboundji Creek"
  )

  # Get family-level gut contents for P. kingsleyae
  ps1_pk_family_counts <- extract_family_counts(ps1_pk)
  ps2_pk_family_counts <- extract_family_counts(ps2_pk)

  # Add metadata
  meta1_sel <- data.frame(sample_data(ps1_pk))
  meta1_sel$SampleID <- rownames(meta1_sel)

  meta2_sel <- data.frame(sample_data(ps2_pk))
  meta2_sel$SampleID <- rownames(meta2_sel)

  ps1_pk_family_counts <- ps1_pk_family_counts %>%
    left_join(meta1_sel %>%
                select(SampleID, Locality_Name, Waveform_Type, Region),
              by = "SampleID")

  ps2_pk_family_counts <- ps2_pk_family_counts %>%
    left_join(meta2_sel %>%
                select(SampleID, Locality_Name, Waveform_Type, Region),
              by = "SampleID")

  # Function to calculate selectivity for an individual fish
  calc_individual_selectivity <- function(sample_id, diet_data, env_data_site, metadata) {

    # Get diet for this individual fish
    diet_individual <- diet_data %>%
      filter(SampleID == sample_id)

    if (nrow(diet_individual) == 0) return(NULL)

    # Calculate selectivity
    selectivity <- calculate_selectivity(
      diet_counts = diet_individual %>% select(Family, Count),
      env_counts = env_data_site %>% select(Family, Count)
    )

    # Add metadata
    meta_row <- metadata[metadata$SampleID == sample_id, ]
    selectivity$SampleID <- sample_id
    selectivity$Locality <- meta_row$Locality_Name
    selectivity$Waveform <- meta_row$Waveform_Type
    selectivity$Region <- meta_row$Region

    return(selectivity)
  }

  # Calculate selectivity for EACH INDIVIDUAL FISH
  selectivity_results_ps1 <- list()
  selectivity_results_ps2 <- list()

  # Get unique samples with locality info
  samples_ps1 <- ps1_pk_family_counts %>%
    distinct(SampleID, Locality_Name, Waveform_Type, Region)

  for (i in 1:nrow(samples_ps1)) {
    sample_id <- samples_ps1$SampleID[i]
    locality <- samples_ps1$Locality_Name[i]

    # Get site name for environmental data
    site_name <- locality_to_site[locality]
    if (is.na(site_name)) next

    # Get environmental data for this site
    env_site <- env_data %>% filter(Site == site_name)
    if (nrow(env_site) == 0) next

    # Calculate selectivity for this individual
    selectivity <- calc_individual_selectivity(
      sample_id = sample_id,
      diet_data = ps1_pk_family_counts,
      env_data_site = env_site,
      metadata = meta1_sel
    )

    if (!is.null(selectivity)) {
      selectivity_results_ps1[[sample_id]] <- selectivity
    }
  }

  # Combine results
  if (length(selectivity_results_ps1) > 0) {
    selectivity_ps1_df <- bind_rows(selectivity_results_ps1)

    cat("PRIMERSET 1 - Selectivity Analysis (Individual-level):\n")
    cat("  Individuals analyzed:", n_distinct(selectivity_ps1_df$SampleID), "\n")
    cat("  Total family-individual combinations:", nrow(selectivity_ps1_df), "\n\n")

    # Summary statistics by Waveform
    cat("Mean Ivlev's E by Waveform:\n")
    selectivity_summary_ps1 <- selectivity_ps1_df %>%
      group_by(Waveform, Region) %>%
      summarise(
        n_individuals = n_distinct(SampleID),
        n_families = n_distinct(Family),
        mean_E = mean(Ivlev_E, na.rm = TRUE),
        mean_abs_E = mean(abs(Ivlev_E), na.rm = TRUE),
        n_preference = sum(Selection_Type == "Preference"),
        n_avoidance = sum(Selection_Type == "Avoidance"),
        .groups = "drop"
      )

    print(selectivity_summary_ps1)

    # Test: Do BP and TP differ in selectivity profiles?
    # Create wide matrix of Ivlev's E values (one row per individual)
    selectivity_wide_ps1 <- selectivity_ps1_df %>%
      select(SampleID, Region, Waveform, Locality, Family, Ivlev_E) %>%
      group_by(SampleID, Region, Waveform, Locality, Family) %>%
      summarise(Ivlev_E = mean(Ivlev_E, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Family, values_from = Ivlev_E, values_fill = 0)
```
